programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Robert Powell stars as Chrissie's father, whom she urges to return to Holby and help Tricia through her illness. However, he proves reluctant, explaining that she doesn't know what really happened between them. Diane attends a seminar, but is appalled to discover it's being held by Dominic Fryer, who offers to boost her career in return for picking up where they left off. Back at the hospital, Zubin confesses to Ric why he didn't tell him about Leo's baby - but can't bring himself to reveal the whole truth(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A host of new staff members graces the wards of Holby following Jude and Matt's departure. Charlie receives a phone call advising there has been a bomb threat in the Towers shopping centre - which is where Baz is heading on her day off. Meanwhile, a middle-aged man falls from a roof and Josh and Liz are forced to deliver a baby in an ambulance(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Jim sinks to a new low when he pawns valuable police evidence to raise the cash he needs to pay off his gambling debts - then promptly blows the lot on a horse. Andrea refuses to abandon her undercover assignment even though Adam is getting uncomfortably close to the truth. Drama, starring Ian McShane, Dudley Sutton, Caroline Langrishe and Susan Fleetwood(n)

programme.title: Bergerac

programme.title: Are You Being Served?

programme.desc: At the behest of a local businessman, unlikely manhunter Hetty fearlessly sets out in search of the thugs responsible for attacking Asian businessmen. She soon discovers that every victim has been a restaurant owner, but then Hetty herself becomes a target of the gang involved(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A teenage prostitute is badly beaten, an elderly woman falls down a flight of stairs, while a pair of neighbours get into a fight over a boundary fence. Brigit Forsyth guest stars(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A road traffic accident turns into a drugs investigation when a quantity of cocaine is found in the crashed vehicle. The driver is dead, but Yvonne is more interested in his passenger, none other than Shirley Moss, who's suffered two broken legs. However, when she begins to question the errant teenager about her continued involvement in drugs, Yvonne soon has reason to believe the youngster isn't the architect of her own downfall - especially after the PC's sister, Beverley, is spotted entering a dealer's flat being staked out by Ken and Suzie. Elsewhere, Mark is upset that his colleagues in SO19 have discovered he is gay, but with the secret now out he throws caution to the wind and decides to take his relationship with Lance one step further - it looks like Sun Hill is about to witness its first gay wedding. With romance in the air, the Christmas party gets into full swing, and new girl Amber certainly enjoys herself - and causes much gossip - by very publicly kissing one of the barm(n)

Burt Kwouk guest stars, with Ian McShane(n)

programme.desc: Lionel finally reaches the end of his tether with busybody secretary Mrs Flack - so Jean hatches a cunning plan to ease her out without causing a scene. Comedy, starring Judi Dench, Geoffrey Palmer and Vivienne Martin(n)

Nathaniel Parker, Sharon Small and Lesley Vickerage star(n)

Detective drama, starring Alun Armstrong, James Bolam, Amanda Redman and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.desc: The Glaswegian cop checks into a health farm, convinced its glamorous owner is guilty of murdering her husband's mistress. programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A shooting incident involving a drug importer stuns the hospital staff. A teenage boy is admitted after drinking vodka, and in determining what prompted him to drink the liquor, Kate discovers an affinity with his mother. Sorcha Cusack stars(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: It's a difficult day for Yvonne as she faces up to the fact that she was wrong to throw Shirley out of the house. However, the youngster eventually accepts her apology and it looks as if, once she has pulled through this latest mishap, the kindly PC will be able to get her life back on track - until fate deals a terrible blow. Meanwhile, with the tensions in his relationship with Andrea beginning to take their toll, Neil is devastated at the news that she has been critically injured. He turns up at the hospital, only to be faced by Smithy, having already filled his shoes. Lance finally accepts Mark's proposal of marriage after a long day of deliberation, while Gina is surprised to catch a glimpse of Irene Radford. She is convinced her eyes are deceiving her - until Jack confides in her that Sun Hill's crime matriarch is indeed back. Michele Austin, Andrew Lancel, Simon Rouse, Roberta Taylor, Lynda Bellingham and Stefan Booth star(n)

programme.desc: Pete gets caught with his trousers down. Hattie feels that Steve's mind should be turned to more romantic matters(n)

When Charlotte sees Lovejoy's caring side towards the 'widow', she feels she may have misjudged his character and finally gives in to his seductive charm. Pete Postlethwaite guest stars, with Ian McShane(n)

The pair also tackle their own health concerns, including Steph's apprehension at exercising while pregnant and Hugh's worry that he's starting to lose his mental sharpness. Hugh then meets a family in Devon struggling with a very common ailment - fussy eating. With mealtimes a battlefield, he wants to discover if a counterintuitive idea may finally bring peace to the dinner table(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Natural history documentary in which spy cameras camouflaged as various creatures go 'undercover' in the animal world, getting up close and personal to film their behaviour. This time, cameras follow Japanese macaques in the snow-covered mountains of Japan, as they gather at steaming hot thermal pools to bathe and socialise. Elsewhere in the cool mountains of Mexico, cameras capture a spectacular gathering of billions of monarch butterflies, and in Bavaria, Germany, Spy Beaver gains unique access to the secretive world of the beaver(n)

programme.title: Crime - Are We Tough Enough?
programme.desc: Lawyers Chris Daw and Ayesha Nayyar spend time with different police forces, looking at how they are dealing with crime(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development of a three-bedroom semi in Darwen in Lancashire, a plot of land in Cromford in Derbyshire and an end terrace in Chatham in Kent(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Roisins sample life in Brisbane for a week, but while Julia's dream is to move to Australia in the hope of a better lifestyle for their children, husband Hussein is not so sure(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A shop owner grapples with a dangerous gunman as his two daughters watch in horror, and a frail 93-year-old has money stolen, but a large number of visitors could be the culprit(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Thomas Forrester and Richard Madley offer expert advice at Southwell racecourse, Nottinghamshire, as two good friends take on a father and son-in-law team. Anita Manning hosts(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ruhma feels out of her depth with a deaf patient, Karen uncovers a patient's racy pastime and Zara upsets Ayesha's enthusiasm for her dissertation(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The Kembleford Ramblers' annual outing takes a tragic turn when the local lord is found strangled, so the cleric gets his walking boots on to solve the crime. Murder mystery, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a rural property shopper who has £250,000 to buy her ideal country home in north Wales and has drafted in her best friend for support. Along the way, the host takes a historic train ride along the beautiful coastline and meets the volunteer train driver who is helping keep Wales's railway heritage on track(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Three farm managers, all critical to the running of their businesses, attend an agricultural awards ceremony in London, where they battle it out for Farm Manager of the Year(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Louise Gostelow and David Harper go antique shopping in Wales, where Louise adds to her ever-growing collection of chairs and David takes a risk with items he knows nothing about(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Without alcohol, Linda suggests a day out with Ollie and Mick to distract herself, but her good mood is short lived. Kathy checks on Bobby but tensions soon escalate between her and Sharon, forcing Bobby to intervene. Iqra tries to reason with Keegan, while Ash and Suki begin to build bridges(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Mary Berry gives the contestants just one hour and 45 minutes to create their ultimate curry night, including two different dishes, a traditional bread and a fried snack. This is followed by Chris Bavin's challenge to rustle something up using an ingredient that features on most people's weekly shopping list, and the cook-off between this week's weakest two contestants. Claudia Winkleman hosts(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Christopher Williams' body is found on a boat out at sea. All evidence points towards a deadly fall, but Jack is bothered by the victim's strange appearance and the fact that his car was abandoned miles out into the woods. Suspicion fall on his wife and friends - but with everyone having an alibi, the police drift further away from the truth. Meanwhile, Jack's daughter Siobhan visits and encourages him to accept Anna's offer of joining her on her travels, leaving the DI with a big decision to make(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Buxton, Derbyshire, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason indulge in more Brexit gossip and serious analysis from Westminster and Brussels(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crime - Are We Tough Enough?
programme.desc: Chris Daw and Ayesha Nayyar bring their evidence to court, as they present their arguments to a select jury of 12 people who have featured across the week. Eventually, the jury leaves to consult in a separate room. There are strong arguments on all sides before the jury members cast their votes(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell meet developers who all have very ambitious plans for their auction buys in Littleover in Derby, Barking in Essex and Congleton in Cheshire. Ideas range from a two-floor extension, creating an extra home and a radical rethink on the use of one of the buildings(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Having visited Australia in a camper van 20 years ago, Emma Howells wants to move her family Down Under. However, husband Gerald is less sure. A trial week gives them the chance to see what life could be like for them in Perth, but will it give the couple answers or drive them further apart? Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: An adventure centre and a garage are burgled, but the businesses' own cameras do not show the thief's face, with the victims turning to street cameras for help. Plus, a partially blind man is deceived at a cashpoint by an unscrupulous thief. Presented by Dominic Littlewood(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the Agricultural Showground on Anglesey, where antique experts Roo Irvine and Richard Madley guide two teams as they search for items of interest and value. Away from the contest, Charlie also takes a trip to discover the history of the oldest cheese in Britain(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al and Daniel clash, Bear's inconvenient secret is finally out, while social influencer Luton Bradley's lifestyle proves not to be so glamorous as it seems(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When Lady Felicia's home comes under threat, Father Brown is forced to seek help from an unlikely ally. Mark Williams and Nancy Carroll star(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton goes house hunting with a couple who want their £950,000 budget to buy them a family home in the West Sussex countryside that will give them a better work-life balance. While there, the presenter heads to the seaside town of Worthing, and finds out how one of the oldest working cinemas in the country has been saved from redevelopment(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The Akiki family, with its biodynamic fruit and vegetables and cheese-making clan the Spierings both hope to sell out of home-grown produce at one of London's oldest farmers' markets in Marylebone. A good day's trade will help the farms survive so every customer counts. Narrated by Denis Lawson(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Louise Gostelow and David Harper charge on the wily, windy moors of West Yorkshire, questing for antiques for their final auction. Louise gets her hands on a very early labour-saving device, as well as an illuminating bit of retro chic. David takes a shine to some theatre seats, but things get complicated when he tries to work out how many he can afford(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic swimming silver medallist Siobhan-Marie O'Connor, England cricketer Jake Ball, Great Britain Fed Cup tennis captain Anne Keothavong and former Wales prop Adam Jones join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon takes Kathy's keys and sneaks into Phil's house - only to be caught red-handed - and Mick worries when Linda is nowhere to be found, unaware she has taken Ollie out of school for the day. Suki settles into Walford, but it soon becomes clear she has an ulterior motive and will go to any lengths to reunite her family. Keegan has a new lease of life and throws himself into work, while Whitney makes a horrifying discovery(n)

programme.title: The Goes Wrong Show
programme.desc: Amateur dramatics group the Cornley Polytechnic Drama Society stages a family saga of lust and betrayal set in the American Deep South. A designer error means that one of the sets has been built at a literal 90 degrees to the vertical. Too expensive to re-stage, the gang decides to turn the cameras on their side and pretend that everything's normal. Gravity, however, has other ideas, and even getting in and out of chairs proves potentially limb-threatening. Comedy, starring Henry Lewis, Jonathan Sayer and Henry Shields(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Jo Brand, Simon Day, Kiri Pritchard-McClean and Henning Wehn join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies(n)

programme.title: King Gary
programme.desc: Knowing that one objection from a neighbour could thwart his plans to build an extension, Gary goes on a full charm-offensive by hosting a 'civilised' dinner party. Denise, an experienced soiree host, tries to take over the proceedings, while Big Gary is only interested in watching the boxing on Gary's new curve television alongside his gatecrashing golfing mates. Meanwhile, Terri uses the party as an opportunity to showcase her new mobile cocktail business venture. Comedy, with Tom Davis and Laura Checkley(n)

programme.title: BBC News Special
programme.desc: Huw Edwards introduces coverage of the moment the United Kingdom leaves the European Union. The departure was made possible after Prime Minister Boris Johnson's Brexit deal was backed by MPs in the wake of the general election. From February, the country enters an 11-month transition period to prepare for its new relationship with the EU(n)

programme.title: BBC News Special
programme.desc: Huw Edwards introduces coverage of the moment the United Kingdom leaves the European Union. The departure was made possible after Prime Minister Boris Johnson's Brexit deal was backed by MPs in the wake of the general election. From February, the country enters an 11-month transition period to prepare for its new relationship with the EU(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Hollywood great Jim Carrey, appearing as Doctor Robotnik in the big-screen adaptation of classic video game Sonic the Hedgehog, and Australian actress Margot Robbie, reprising her role as Harley Quinn in superhero film Birds of Prey. British star Daniel Kaluuya and newcomer Jodie Turner-Smith are also on the guest list, discussing romantic drama Queen & Slim. Plus, Lewis Capaldi performs his current single, Before You Go(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Louise Gostelow and David Harper scour Northern Ireland for hidden gems in a soft-top beetle, and discover the stories of legendary heroes and a campaigner for social justice(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Cattle farmers and their protegees bring their finest cows to compete at the Driffield Show, hoping to win the much-coveted red rosettes(n)

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Nadeem Islam travels to Northern Ireland to see the Giant's Causeway, Belfast murals and Titanic Museum. He also meets locals to talk about the Troubles. With voiceover(n)

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley with a report on the counterfeit contact lenses that can make your eyesight worse, and the fake waste disposal companies that could cost consumers a fortune. Elsewhere, Michelle shows how to identify fake emails and dangerous links online, and reveals how criminals can even embed fake messages into a legitimate text chain on your phone(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day. Includes live coverage of Prime Minister's Questions from midday.(n)

programme.title: Get Away for Winter
programme.desc: Sally and Jan want to swap the cold of London for sunshine in Ibiza, and are shown four rental properties on the Spanish island by local property expert Hattie(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott celebrates dishes from across the Channel, getting inspiration from France, Italy and Spain. The menu includes the Greedy Italians' linguine, James Martin's Spanish sardines, a chicken dish from Raymond Blanc, and a Bake Off masterclass on how to make a classic Greek spanakopita(n)

programme.title: Indian Ocean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The third leg of the adventurer's journey takes him from the south of Kenya to the Horn of Africa. Along the way he joins a Ugandan peace-keeping force in Mogadishu, the capital of war-torn Somalia, which turns out to be the most dangerous leg of his journey as he ends up under fire on the front line. In contrast, he finds neighbouring Somaliland a stable and democratic home to thousands of Somalis fleeing fighting and famine. In Kenya's Tana Delta, he meets villagers facing eviction from an area that the government is turning over to sugar cane production for bio-fuels(n)

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Katherine Downes presents action from day 10 of the opening Grand Slam tournament of the year, which takes place at Melbourne Park, featuring the concluding quarter-finals(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Emma Willis is the latest to take part in the popular genealogy documentary, in which famous faces trace their family trees. The TV presenter, who considers herself a true Brummie, is keen to learn more about her family in Birmingham, tracing back to Victorian times and her hard-working ancestor James Gretton, a local merchant. The Big Brother host then learns that her father's grandmother was born in Ireland. Surprised to hear of this new line of ancestry, Emma heads across to Dublin to find out more - but she is disturbed by what she learns(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts Thomas Plant and David Harper are on hand at the Watts Gallery in Surrey to find a selection of objects to take to auction(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Radzi Chinyanganya, Hal Cruttenden, Kerry Godliman and Ebony Rainford-Brent test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On this leg of his trip in Thailand, Michael Portillo tries his hand at the art of umbrella-making and learns about northern Thai cookery. He explores the walled and moated city of Chiang Mai, former capital of the Lanna Kingdom before it was annexed by the King of Siam. Travelling south to Lampang, he discovers the former centre of the teak wood business and investigates how successive Thai kings preserved their independence from the rival colonial powers of Britain and France(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, including the latest on Brexit with Andrew Neil and guests(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar goes on a ramble that straddles the north and south sides of Calderdale, stopping along the way to sketch and read poetry. She begins in the village of Heptonstall, rambles past Hell Hole and along the canal in Hebden Bridge, before climbing to the Stoodley Pike peace monument(n)

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams investigate rumours that swallows are present in Sussex in January. They also look at why there's been a severe lack of snow in Scotland this winter, and share an incredible spectacle in the form of flocks of brent geese migrating from Northern Canada to the urban streets of Dublin(n)

programme.title: Good Omens
programme.desc: Aziraphale and Crowley's friendship across the ages is revealed, as they are seen in the Garden of Eden, at the time of Noah's Ark, in Jerusalem, Ancient Rome, Arthurian Britain, at Shakespeare's Globe Theatre, in a prison in Revolutionary France, Victorian London, getting mixed up with a Nazi spy ring in the Second World War, and in London's 'Swinging Sixties'. Meanwhile in the present day, Agnes Nutter's descendant arrives on her world-saving mission. Starring Michael Sheen, David Tennant, Adria Arjona and Miranda Richardson(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Last week saw the return of the comedy show in which three comedians take the stage of the Hammersmith Apollo in west London. The premise is simple. One comic acts as host, warming up the audience before introducing short sets by two other stand-ups. Future editions promise the likes of Ed Byrne, Rob Beckett, Joel Dommett, Henning Wehn, Lucy Porter, but tonight Nish Kumar plays host, before handing over to David O'Doherty and Luisa Omielan(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: Belsen: Our Story
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the concentration camp, featuring personal accounts from the few remaining survivors and archive footage shot by the British forces that liberated them. Bergen-Belsen was used to hold prisoners evacuated from camps that had fallen to the Allied advance, leading its population to increase to nearly 60,000 by the winter of 1944. Thousands died at the camp from starvation and disease, their bodies left unburied. The British and Canadian forces who discovered the camp were left with no choice but to burn it to the ground(n)

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Nadeem Islam travels to Northern Ireland to see the Giant's Causeway, Belfast murals and Titanic Museum. He also meets locals to talk about the Troubles. With voiceover(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: In America, it's an epidemic. Now new evidence from Public Health England raises concern about the UK's use of prescription opioids. Dr Michael Mosley investigates this growing problem, meeting patients struggling with addiction and GPs fighting a constant battle to help those suffering from chronic pain. He also uncovers worrying evidence of people abusing over-the-counter opioids and discovers how easy it is to buy strong opioids online. Michael also considers why people are consuming twice the amount of opioids as 20 years ago, and asks the crucial question - do they actually work?(n)

programme.title: The Speedshop
programme.desc: Following custom bike builder Titch Cormack and his team as they take on mechanical challenges to convert broken and battered vehicles into beautiful, bespoke machines. In the first edition, Titch receives a visit from a customer who needs his help. Parachute regiment soldier Chris, who just a few weeks earlier had his right leg amputated, wants Titch to build him a specially adapted motorcycle, but there is a twist - he wants to take it on a challenging off-road ride, in an attempt to prove to himself that 'he can still be the man he used to be' and that his new amputation won't hold him back(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Louise Gostelow and David Harper scour Lancashire and Cumbria for items to sell at auction in Runcorn. On the way, Louise learns about the method for making Lancashire Cheese(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two dedicated farming families hope to impress potential buyers at The Scottish Game Fair with farm produced potato vodka and a selection of wild game burgers(n)

programme.title: Baby Chimp Rescue
programme.desc: New arrivals keep arriving at the chimpanzee orphanage in Liberia, bringing Jimmy and Jenny Desmond and their team to breaking point as they find themselves providing around-the-clock care for 38 rescued babies. After losing one of the chimps to illness, they are more determined than ever to build them a sanctuary in the forest where they can live like wild chimps. To prepare for the move, the orphans are learning to make their own nests and get their first taste of life in the forest(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Andrew Neil and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Get Away for Winter
programme.desc: A couple have their hearts set on renting a property in Cadiz, Spain, but availability is a problem as they are going at a busy time. Property expert Ali is on hand to guide them(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives for spicy and exotic dishes, including tapas by James Martin, Paul Hollywood's tortilla tower, a Jamaican ginger cake from the Hairy Bikers and Ainsley's own jerk pork(n)

programme.title: Indian Ocean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The fourth leg of the adventurer's journey takes him from Oman to the Maldives. He starts in the Strait of Hormuz, where oil from the Gulf is shipped through the narrow channel, then moves on to Mumbai, the ocean's biggest port. In the Maldives, Simon finds arguably the most beautiful collection of tropical coral islands in the world - but the fragile underwater environment is a barometer for the changing nature of the sea, as he witnesses how bleaching has damaged the coral and an entire island has been manufactured as a landfill dump to deal with the problem of rubbish(n)

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents action from day 11 of the Grand Slam tournament, which takes place at Melbourne Park, featuring both women's semi-finals and the opening men's last-four match(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Lulu has always wanted to get to the bottom of a family mystery. She knows that her mother, the middle child of seven, was the only one to be given up by her birth parents and raised by another family. But she has no idea why. Travelling home to Glasgow, she uncovers the real-life Romeo and Juliet story of her Catholic grandfather and Protestant grandmother's love affair across the city's strict sectarian divide. But as she digs deeper, she discovers dark secrets that force her to reassess her mum's story(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts Catherine Southon and Katherine Higgins are at the Royal Marines Museum in Hampshire, where a 19th-century Japanese woodblock and a pristine pocket watch stimulate interest. Meanwhile, presenter Paul Martin learns how Portsmouth influenced the Victorian author Charles Dickens(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Radzi Chinyanganya, Hal Cruttenden, Kerry Godliman and Ebony Rainford-Brent test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo journeys through Thailand, stopping to help at a hospital for elephants wounded by landmines and visiting ruined temples in the former Siamese capital of Ayutthaya(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The Rev Richard Coles and actor Joe McFadden scour Scotland for antiques with David Harper and Philip Serrell, with a nailbiting auction ensuing after both teams buy near-identical items. Before the auction south of the border in Newcastle, Richard and David play football at one of Scotland's most famous clubs, while Joe and Philip end up going round in circles deep underground(n)

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams present more wildlife reports live from their base in the Cairngorms. They reveal the science behind why ducks don't die of cold, hear about the mini beasts captured on camera by a passionate amateur photographer and share the mass spectacle of 30,000 redwings roosting in a remote wood in Lancashire's Ribble Valley(n)

programme.title: Travels in Euroland with Ed Balls
programme.desc: The former MP continues his journey across Europe in Poland and Germany. In Leczna, eastern Poland, he meets coal miners who explain the importance of an industry that is now under threat from the EU. Crossing the border to Germany, Ed heads to Dorfchemnitz, which in the 2017 national election returned the highest vote in the country for the far-right Alternative for Germany party. He also visits Bremen, a city that has felt the impact of Angela Merkel's controversial policy to take on more refugees during the migrant crisis than any other country in Europe(n)

programme.title: Mum
programme.desc: Cathy and her family decamp to the English countryside to celebrate Derek's birthday. This time, Cathy and Michael get ready to go for a walk in the countryside, but Jason has concerns. Starring Lesley Manville, Sam Swainsbury and Peter Mullen(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: Win the Wilderness: Alaska
programme.desc: Six couples are challenged to prove their survival skills in Alaska's harsh wilderness, with the most successful pair winning a remarkable home miles from the nearest road, which was built from scratch by its original owners. In the first episode, they receive a crash course in what to do when encountering a bear before being sent into the woods to gather material and build shelters. They must then fell trees, make a fire and brave the freezing waters of Lost Lake(n)

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: A brother and sister team fish for oysters in a traditional wooden sailing boat on the Fal estuary - but this is not enough to support them, so now they plan to trade it in for a small trawler. Further down river in St Mawes, a veteran trawler captain reflects on a past brush with death and the changing face of his home town, as working boats give way to yachts(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood joins the coastguard of Corpus Christi, Texas, taking to the skies with a helicopter patrol and joining a boat crew looking for illegal fishing and smuggling(n)

programme.title: Belsen: Our Story
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the concentration camp, featuring personal accounts from the few remaining survivors and archive footage shot by the British forces that liberated them. Bergen-Belsen was used to hold prisoners evacuated from camps that had fallen to the Allied advance, leading its population to increase to nearly 60,000 by the winter of 1944. Thousands died at the camp from starvation and disease, their bodies left unburied. The British and Canadian forces who discovered the camp were left with no choice but to burn it to the ground(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Louise Gostelow and David Harper go antique shopping in Wales, where Louise adds to her ever-growing collection of chairs and David takes a risk with items he knows nothing about(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Three farm managers, all critical to the running of their businesses, attend an agricultural awards ceremony in London, where they battle it out for Farm Manager of the Year(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The first three remaining semi-finalists battle it out for a place in final, and are given the chance to work alongside one of the UK's most exciting chefs, Gareth Ward. First, the chefs are challenged to create a dish of their own design for Gareth to taste, inspired by his own cooking. The competitors will then take on the daunting task of joining Gareth's brigade, taking responsibility for two dishes each during a highly-pressurised service, before returning to the MasterChef kitchen to present a dish of their own design to the judges(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Get Away for Winter
programme.desc: Newcastle couple Josie and Laurence enlist the help of local property expert Dave in Lanzarote to find a perfect rental home for them to spend part of the winter. But will any of the four properties give them everything they want, and will they make the move south for the winter? Gloria Hunniford narrates(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of classic recipes from some of the BBC's best-loved television chefs. Ainsley explores perfect pairings, ingredients that always work together beautifully. The menu includes Rachel Khoo's lavender chicken, Lorraine Pascale's squash and sweet potato lasagne, Ainsley's own duck and honey dish and an apple and cinnamon cake from Mary Berry(n)

programme.title: Indian Ocean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer reaches Sri Lanka, whose strategic location and tropical spices made it a target for invaders and colonisers for centuries. In the north he visits the scenes of vicious battles between the Tamil minority and the Sri Lankan army, traumatic events from which the population is still recovering. On his way to Bangladesh, he hitches a ride on a trawler, highlighting one of the Indian Ocean's fastest-growing industries - providing prawns for the West. But as he reveals, it comes at a price for the environment(n)

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents action from day 12 of the Grand Slam tournament, which takes place at Melbourne Park, featuring the concluding men's singles semi-final(n)

programme.title: Indian Ocean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer begins the final leg of his journey on the northern tip of Sumatra in western Indonesia, near the epicentre of the 2004 Boxing Day tsunami, finding out how the province of Banda Aceh has undergone many changes since the disaster. He also explores the illegal trade in exotic pets in the Indonesian capital of Jakarta, before heading for Australia, the final country on his epic journey. There he visits the unspoilt wilderness of the Kimberley region, meets a crocodile hunter and goes fishing with natives campaigning to stop the construction of a giant gas plant(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antique experts Catherine Southon and David Fletcher scour a crowd at Ushaw in Co Durham to find objects to take off to auction. Catherine uncovers a large collection of glassware and stoneware dug up in the north east of England, while David finds an American money box in the shape of a bulldog. Host Paul Martin visits the Oriental Museum at Durham University to find out about the collection of Malcolm McDonald, a British diplomat who was passionate about Asian ceramics(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Radzi Chinyanganya, Hal Cruttenden, Kerry Godliman and Ebony Rainford-Brent test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games, with the week's overall champion set to be crowned(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo visits the Thai capital of Bangkok, mid-way through his rail tour of the country, where he learns how the late-19th-century King Rama V built a grand palace and introduced railways to the country. He discovers the fine terminus commissioned by the king and meets a historian and designer, who shares family roots with the present royal dynasty. Michael also learns that King Rama V's love of steam engines was inspired by Queen Victoria(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Documentary following the ordinary men and women who volunteer with the Royal National Lifeboat Institution, risking their lives every day to save others who are in danger(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Helen Flanagan and Jorgie Porter scour some of Scotland's finest antique shops in the hope of finding items that will make the biggest profit at auction in Stirlingshire. Jorgie takes a detour to hit an ace at on a very historic tennis court, while Helen takes a break from the shops to learn about tartan and kilts. Experts Philip Serrell and James Braxton try to keep the duo on the straight and narrow(n)

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams review the extraordinary events that have taken place this week as well as look back over an eventful year in the Cairngorms. With three species of Egrets now breeding in Britain, butterflies on the wing year-round and record-breaking temperatures in the Scottish Highlands, they also explores what winters may be like in the future(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig looks at some 'Queens', inviting Sarah Millican, David Mitchell and Colin Lane to join regular panellist Alan Davies to face her fiendish questions. As usual, the host doesn't particularly care whether the answers are right or not, so long as they are interesting(n)

programme.title: Road to Brexit
programme.desc: Matt Berry offers his unique take on Brexit in this one-off comedy special to mark the upcoming Article 50 deadline. Reuniting with collaborator Arthur Mathews for the first time since Toast of London, Berry plays rogue historian Michael Squeamish, who's on a mission to discover the origins of Brexit, and offer some interesting opinions on Britain's current plight along the way. Through creative use of archive footage and filmed interviews, the programme unashamedly plays fast and loose with the facts to create a surreal whistle-stop tour of Britain's relationship with Europe(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: In Glasgow, presenter Mary Beard and a panel of guests discuss how culture is responding to the issue of migration. They question how writing and drama can contribute towards an essential conversation about this most topical and controversial of issues, whether migration a valid topic for comedy or satire and how attitudes towards migration reflect a society itself(n)

programme.title: Romeo & Juliet (2013)
programme.desc: The children of two feuding noble houses fall in love, and marry in secret with the aid of a wily priest who hopes to reconcile the two families. However, when the conflict flares up again with fatal consequences, it sets in motion a chain of events that threatens to tear the lovers apart. Adaptation of Shakespeare's tragedy, starring Douglas Booth, Hailee Steinfeld and Paul Giamatti(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Handmade
programme.desc: Filmed in an industrial forge lit only by the welding torch and burning metal, this programme examines the process involved in the crafting of a steel knife. Bladesmith Owen Bush uses a variety of techniques, both ancient and modern, and takes his time over the complex skill of shaping and blending the steel into a knife(n)

programme.title: World's Busiest Railway 2015
programme.desc: Dan Snow, Anita Rani, Robert Llewellyn and John Sergeant go behind the scenes at Mumbai's Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus, revealing the science, systems and staff responsible for keeping this supersized transport system running to schedule. In the opening edition, Dan, Anita and Robert experience conditions known as 'super dense crush load' as they ride on the world's busiest commuter trains, while John heads to Darjeeling's steam-powered hill railway to examine the historical connection between tea and trains(n)

programme.title: The Brexit Storm: Laura Kuenssberg's Inside Story
programme.desc: With Westminster in crisis and the Prime Minister striving to get her deal through Parliament, the BBC's political editor takes viewers through every twist and turn of the most extraordinary political story of recent times. Cameras follow key politicians as well as Laura herself as she reports on exceptional events, from Theresa May's doomed efforts to secure backing for her Chequers plan to cabinet resignations, backbench plots and historic parliamentary defeats, as well as a national campaign for a second referendum(n)

programme.title: The Brexit Storm Continues: Laura Kuenssberg's Inside Story
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the tumultuous events of Boris Johnson's Conservative government. The programme follows his appointment as leader of the Conservative party, attempts to secure a Brexit deal with the European Union, the attempted proroguing of parliament, an act subsequently declared unlawful, and the eventual calling of a general election. Featuring contributions from MPs on all sides, who reveal the pressure they have come under from an increasingly divided and frustrated electorate(n)

programme.title: Art, Passion & Power: The Story of the Royal Collection
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon explores the history of the Royal Collection - the dazzling array of art and decorative objects collected by kings and queens over the past 500 years, and today owned by the Queen as sovereign. The first programme focuses on the works acquired by the collection's founders - Henry VIII and Charles I. Henry VIII tried to overwhelm with magnificence, believing that great art projected power. For Charles I art was a way to compete with other kings through taste, and Andrew reveals how this doomed monarch became the greatest royal collector in British history(n)

programme.title: Britain and the Sea
programme.desc: David Dimbleby continues his voyage around Britain, sailing his yacht Rocket along coastal regions of the South East, the first point of attack for enemy forces and therefore essential to the nation's defence throughout history. Along the way, he takes a tour of HMS Victory, Nelson's flagship vessel from the Battle of Trafalgar, lovingly preserved in dry dock in Portsmouth. He also meets a carpenter who still makes wooden figureheads, discovers how a chamber pot acted as propaganda in the battle against Napoleon and reveals the way abstract art was used to deceive German submarine pilots(n)

programme.title: How to Get Ahead
programme.desc: The three-part documentary continues as writer and broadcaster Stephen Smith explores Renaissance Florence under the reign of Grand Duke Cosimo Medici, whose fledgling court prized the finer things in life. Successful courtiers had to have brains as well as brawn, and some looked to theorists like Niccolo Machiavelli for underhand ways to get ahead, while enlightened polymaths turned their minds to the heavens - and to ice-cream(n)

programme.title: World's Busiest Railway 2015
programme.desc: Dan Snow, Anita Rani, Robert Llewellyn and John Sergeant go behind the scenes at Mumbai's Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus, revealing the science, systems and staff responsible for keeping this supersized transport system running to schedule. In the opening edition, Dan, Anita and Robert experience conditions known as 'super dense crush load' as they ride on the world's busiest commuter trains, while John heads to Darjeeling's steam-powered hill railway to examine the historical connection between tea and trains(n)

programme.title: A Very British Romance with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian delves into 300 years of love's rituals to uncover the forces shaping the happy ever after, revealing how people's most intimate thoughts and feelings have been affected by social, political and cultural ideas. Lucy begins in the Georgian age, when - after centuries of marriage being as much about business as love - women began to have an unprecedented degree of romantic freedom(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: The Pennine Way
programme.desc: Adventurer Paul Rose pulls on his hiking boots to explore the 268-mile Pennine Way, to mark the 50th anniversary of Britain's first national trail being officially opened. His first leg takes him from Edale to Calderdale, and along the way he tells the story of Tom Stephenson, who fought landowners and governments to win public access to the full route. He also meets author and director Barrie Rutter, who recalls some of the literary greats who have lived nearby, including Ted Hughes and the Brontes(n)

programme.title: The Great British Year
programme.desc: The documentary follows wildlife throughout autumn, when the fading sun brings a change of energy to the landscape, while creatures prepare for shorter, darker days. Sika deer stags battle for supremacy, dormice curl up for their winter snooze, barnacle geese arrive from the Arctic and baby seals face the tempestuous waves, while salmon complete their epic journey to spawn before they die. By December, birds huddle together as the first snowflakes fall, and the seasonal cycle begins again. Narrated by Joseph Fiennes(n)

programme.title: Life Cinematic
programme.desc: The director of American Beauty, Skyfall and Revolutionary Road talks to Edith Bowman about the movies that have influenced his career. His choices range from Blue Velvet, Taxi Driver and The Godfather II to Lost In Translation. Sam also provides an insight into the making of his most recent film, 1917, and reflects on his early introduction to cinema(n)

programme.title: Revolutionary Road (2008)
programme.desc: Married couple Frank and April Wheeler live a picture-perfect life on the surface, but in truth are growing frustrated with their lot and feel stifled by their situation. When April suggests the family moves to France to start a new life, Frank is persuaded it is a good idea, though when he is offered a stable job with a lucrative pay packet he changes his mind, leaving April a desperate figure longing for an escape. Sam Mendes' 1950s-set drama, starring Kate Winslet, Leonardo DiCaprio, Michael Shannon and Kathy Bates(n)

programme.title: How We Built Britain
programme.desc: David Dimbleby examines some of Britain's most extraordinary architecture, exploring the social history of the nation and revealing the stories behind each area's structures. He begins in East Anglia, where he visits Ely Cathedral and investigates how the Normans used their revolutionary building skills in the 11th century to demonstrate their power over the local population(n)

programme.title: How to Get Ahead
programme.desc: Writer and broadcaster Stephen Smith explores the flamboyant court of French monarch Louis XIV at the Palace of Versailles, where he surrounded himself with aristocrats, artists, interior designers, gardeners, wig-makers, chefs and musicians. Ambitious courtiers scrambled to get closer to the king, but the bedchamber seemed to provide a more unseemly path to power(n)

programme.title: A History of Art in Three Colours
programme.desc: Art historian James Fox concludes his examination of the three colours he considers most important to human history by arguing that white, traditionally a symbol of virtue and purity, was warped into a shade that exposed the darkest human instincts. He explores why the colour fascinated 18th-century potter Josiah Wedgwood and art historian Johann Joachim Winckelmann, and examines how in the 20th century it came to be linked with the Italian dictator Mussolini and fascism(n)

programme.title: Life Cinematic
programme.desc: The director of American Beauty, Skyfall and Revolutionary Road talks to Edith Bowman about the movies that have influenced his career. His choices range from Blue Velvet, Taxi Driver and The Godfather II to Lost In Translation. Sam also provides an insight into the making of his most recent film, 1917, and reflects on his early introduction to cinema(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mark Goodier and Andy Crane present the edition first shown on February 16, featuring performances by Def Leppard, Simple Minds, Gloria Estefan, Poison, Edie Brickell and the New Bohemians, Pop Will Eat Itself, Debbie Gibson, Texas, Sam Brown, the Style Council, Marc Almond and Gene Pitney, and S-Express(n)

programme.title: Sound of Musicals with Neil Brand
programme.desc: The writer and composer looks at the rise of the all-conquering contemporary blockbuster, from Evita to Les Miserables and The Lion King. He also sees how these mega-musicals have met with unexpected competition from quirky hits such as The Rocky Horror Show and Avenue Q. Contributors include Tim Rice, Alan Menkin and Robert Lopez(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes and Susie Mathis present the edition first shown on February 23, featuring performances by S-Express, Michael Jackson, Edie Brickell and the New Bohemians, Rick Astley, Tyree featuring Kool Rock Stead, Living in a Box, Tone Loc, Dusty Springfield, Hue and Cry, Michael Ball, Simple Minds, and Bananarama and Lananeeneenoonoo(n)

programme.title: Stewart Copeland's Adventures in Music
programme.desc: The performer explores the transcendental powers of music and how certain sounds have the ability to move transport the mind and even help people briefly 'escape the world'. Stuart travels to Morocco to discover the polyrhythms of Gnawa and back to Wells Cathedral to understand the mechanics of choral polyphony. He meets Kanye West collaborator Caroline Shaw to examine melody and the effect of the human voice, CeCe Winans to discuss the roots of Gospel, takes a gong bath in New York and visits minimalist master Steve Reich to unpack the trance inducing powers of repetition(n)

programme.title: Buddy Holly: Rave On
programme.desc: Rock 'n' roll started with Elvis, but some believe that pop music started with Buddy Holly and the Crickets. As a songwriter, Holly revolutionised the genre by introducing dynamic new rhythms and unpredictable melodies beyond its traditional blues roots. In his songs written and recorded in the late '50s, it is already possible to hear the beat group sound of the 1960s, and beyond. This documentary tells the story of Holly's tragically short life and career through interviews with those who knew him and worked with him. Contributors include Don McLean, Paul Anka, Don Everly, Hank Marvin and Bob Harris(n)

programme.title: Reginald D Hunter's Songs of the South
programme.desc: The London-based comedian continues his exploration of the American South through its songs both past and present in Alabama, where he attends a Lynyrd Skynyrd gig and learns about the city of Muscle Shoals' role in the development of soul. Reg then heads to his home state of Georgia, where he visits Athens - where rock bands the B52s and REM originated - and Atlanta, the birthplace of civil rights leader Martin Luther King and rapper Ludacris. Featuring contributions by Arrested Development, St Paul and the Broken Bones, Clarence Carter and Sharon Jones(n)

programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po run around in circles in Home Dome. The Noo-noo joins in and the Teletubbies have fun chasing(n)

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Ball. Babies and their grown-ups play with balls and enjoy songs and story time together(n)

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: Pablo finds the new sofa difficult to accept, which makes it upset(n)

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Four-year-old Ottalie is visited by Grandad and Grandma on the farm. They help Ottalie pick apples from the tree, before Ottalie and Grandma get to work making a tasty apple sponge for pudding(n)

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Gem and her teams of Swashbucklers try to win her jewels back from pirates Cook, Line and Sinker. Game show for children(n)

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Norrie is upset when a leaf falls from a tree, and Roly finds an acorn stuck to his bottom, prompting the squirrels to search for more to share out(n)

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: The crew tries to rescue Peso when he becomes trapped inside a giant jelly(n)

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day heads to the rainforests of Borneo in search of a treeshrew and its favourite food. Along the way, he encounters a tree snake and some hungry wild pigs(n)

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie visits a farm to see where eggs come from and then goes to a mill to see how kernels of wheat are ground and turned into flower(n)

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: The friends venture to Rocky Island, in search of the mysterious 'Old Rusty' mentioned in Peter's father's journal(n)

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Grandmaster Glitch is drilling into the ground below the ocean near Academy Island, looking for bubbly mud that is heated up by an underground volcano(n)

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: When Gramps comes to babysit, he brings Trevor and Violet along too. During a game of hide-and-sing the dogs hide next door, causing confusion and chaos for poor Mrs Hobbs(n)

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Tiny, Small, the Froglets and Baby Dragon have great fun playing games with the Cloud, but when Major calls them in for bed, the Cloud is left alone and sad. Michael Palin narrates(n)

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Pontipines take their dinner on the Ninky Nonk, but a ride round the garden results in a mix-up with the food(n)

programme.title: Eddie Butler's Six Nations
programme.desc: The presenter looks ahead to the championship, speaking to the likes of new Wales head coach Wayne Pivac, his star outside-half Dan Biggar and former Ireland captain Jamie Heaslip about preparations for the tournament. Eddie also looks back at some of the most memorable days in the competition's history(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic hockey gold medallist Sam Quek, Great Britain 400m great Roger Black, Welsh rugby union star Gareth Anscombe and world triathlon champion Jonny Brownlee join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Natural history documentary in which spy cameras camouflaged as various creatures go 'undercover' in the animal world, getting up close and personal to film their behaviour. This time, cameras follow Japanese macaques in the snow-covered mountains of Japan, as they gather at steaming hot thermal pools to bathe and socialise. Elsewhere in the cool mountains of Mexico, cameras capture a spectacular gathering of billions of monarch butterflies, and in Bavaria, Germany, Spy Beaver gains unique access to the secretive world of the beaver(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad and Bethan Rhys Roberts present a weekly show featuring stories and in-depth interviews about what is happening in Wales and why it matters(n)

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Former couples are reunited to talk about their break-ups. Chantelle wants Tevin to forgive her for sleeping with his best friend, and Rachel tries to win back Niraj(n)

programme.title: My Mate's a Bad Date
programme.desc: Oloni helps Dami, a 24-year-old with a history of dating failures, trying to help him own up to his disastrous habits(n)

programme.title: First & Last

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Roisins sample life in Brisbane for a week, but while Julia's dream is to move to Australia in the hope of a better lifestyle for their children, husband Hussein is not so sure(n)

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway tackles the Black Mountains in the Brecon Beacons. Starting his walk in the small village of Bwlch, he explores the history of the Rhiangoll Valley and the village of Cwmdu, before hitting the hills to follow the Beacons Way, up and over and back to Bwlch. His next challenge is to climb one of Wales's super-mountains - Snowdonia's Glyder Fawr at over 3,200ft(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Thomas Forrester and Richard Madley offer expert advice at Southwell racecourse, Nottinghamshire, as two good friends take on a father and son-in-law team. Anita Manning hosts(n)

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ruhma feels out of her depth with a deaf patient, Karen uncovers a patient's racy pastime and Zara upsets Ayesha's enthusiasm for her dissertation(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The Kembleford Ramblers' annual outing takes a tragic turn when the local lord is found strangled, so the cleric gets his walking boots on to solve the crime. Murder mystery, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a rural property shopper who has £250,000 to buy her ideal country home in north Wales and has drafted in her best friend for support. Along the way, the host takes a historic train ride along the beautiful coastline and meets the volunteer train driver who is helping keep Wales's railway heritage on track(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Three farm managers, all critical to the running of their businesses, attend an agricultural awards ceremony in London, where they battle it out for Farm Manager of the Year(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Louise Gostelow and David Harper go antique shopping in Wales, where Louise adds to her ever-growing collection of chairs and David takes a risk with items he knows nothing about(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Without alcohol, Linda suggests a day out with Ollie and Mick to distract herself, but her good mood is short lived. Kathy checks on Bobby but tensions soon escalate between her and Sharon, forcing Bobby to intervene. Iqra tries to reason with Keegan, while Ash and Suki begin to build bridges(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Mary Berry gives the contestants just one hour and 45 minutes to create their ultimate curry night, including two different dishes, a traditional bread and a fried snack. This is followed by Chris Bavin's challenge to rustle something up using an ingredient that features on most people's weekly shopping list, and the cook-off between this week's weakest two contestants. Claudia Winkleman hosts(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Christopher Williams' body is found on a boat out at sea. All evidence points towards a deadly fall, but Jack is bothered by the victim's strange appearance and the fact that his car was abandoned miles out into the woods. Suspicion fall on his wife and friends - but with everyone having an alibi, the police drift further away from the truth. Meanwhile, Jack's daughter Siobhan visits and encourages him to accept Anna's offer of joining her on her travels, leaving the DI with a big decision to make(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Buxton, Derbyshire, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason indulge in more Brexit gossip and serious analysis from Westminster and Brussels(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crime - Are We Tough Enough?
programme.desc: Chris Daw and Ayesha Nayyar bring their evidence to court, as they present their arguments to a select jury of 12 people who have featured across the week. Eventually, the jury leaves to consult in a separate room. There are strong arguments on all sides before the jury members cast their votes(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell meet developers who all have very ambitious plans for their auction buys in Littleover in Derby, Barking in Essex and Congleton in Cheshire. Ideas range from a two-floor extension, creating an extra home and a radical rethink on the use of one of the buildings(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Having visited Australia in a camper van 20 years ago, Emma Howells wants to move her family Down Under. However, husband Gerald is less sure. A trial week gives them the chance to see what life could be like for them in Perth, but will it give the couple answers or drive them further apart? Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the Agricultural Showground on Anglesey, where antique experts Roo Irvine and Richard Madley guide two teams as they search for items of interest and value. Away from the contest, Charlie also takes a trip to discover the history of the oldest cheese in Britain(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al and Daniel clash, Bear's inconvenient secret is finally out, while social influencer Luton Bradley's lifestyle proves not to be so glamorous as it seems(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When Lady Felicia's home comes under threat, Father Brown is forced to seek help from an unlikely ally. Mark Williams and Nancy Carroll star(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton goes house hunting with a couple who want their £950,000 budget to buy them a family home in the West Sussex countryside that will give them a better work-life balance. While there, the presenter heads to the seaside town of Worthing, and finds out how one of the oldest working cinemas in the country has been saved from redevelopment(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The Akiki family, with its biodynamic fruit and vegetables and cheese-making clan the Spierings both hope to sell out of home-grown produce at one of London's oldest farmers' markets in Marylebone. A good day's trade will help the farms survive so every customer counts. Narrated by Denis Lawson(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Louise Gostelow and David Harper charge on the wily, windy moors of West Yorkshire, questing for antiques for their final auction. Louise gets her hands on a very early labour-saving device, as well as an illuminating bit of retro chic. David takes a shine to some theatre seats, but things get complicated when he tries to work out how many he can afford(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Six Nations Sin Bin
programme.desc: Gabby Logan and Gareth Thomas are joined by opera star Bryn Terfel, comedian Hugh Dennis and former Wales captain Rachel Taylor, as they take an entertaining look at Wales' prospects at this year's Six Nations(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen rewinds the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television, here discovering how to have a good time. It's a story of horn blowing, bog snorkelling and Elvis impersonators, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon takes Kathy's keys and sneaks into Phil's house - only to be caught red-handed - and Mick worries when Linda is nowhere to be found, unaware she has taken Ollie out of school for the day. Suki settles into Walford, but it soon becomes clear she has an ulterior motive and will go to any lengths to reunite her family. Keegan has a new lease of life and throws himself into work, while Whitney makes a horrifying discovery(n)

programme.title: The Goes Wrong Show
programme.desc: Amateur dramatics group the Cornley Polytechnic Drama Society stages a family saga of lust and betrayal set in the American Deep South. A designer error means that one of the sets has been built at a literal 90 degrees to the vertical. Too expensive to re-stage, the gang decides to turn the cameras on their side and pretend that everything's normal. Gravity, however, has other ideas, and even getting in and out of chairs proves potentially limb-threatening. Comedy, starring Henry Lewis, Jonathan Sayer and Henry Shields(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Jo Brand, Simon Day, Kiri Pritchard-McClean and Henning Wehn join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies(n)

programme.title: The Tuckers
programme.desc: Billy naively thinks he and Bobby can make some easy money from a 'simple' house clearance. Nats is rescued by Bobby - or so she thinks. Comedy about a family of chancers, low on cash but high on spirit - determined to survive through any means necessary. Joshua McCord, Ben McGregor and Alexandria Riley star(n)

programme.title: BBC News Special
programme.desc: Huw Edwards introduces coverage of the moment the United Kingdom leaves the European Union. The departure was made possible after Prime Minister Boris Johnson's Brexit deal was backed by MPs in the wake of the general election. From February, the country enters an 11-month transition period to prepare for its new relationship with the EU(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Hollywood great Jim Carrey, appearing as Doctor Robotnik in the big-screen adaptation of classic video game Sonic the Hedgehog, and Australian actress Margot Robbie, reprising her role as Harley Quinn in superhero film Birds of Prey. programme.desc: Alistair Appleton goes house hunting with a couple who want their £950,000 budget to buy them a family home in the West Sussex countryside that will give them a better work-life balance. While there, the presenter heads to the seaside town of Worthing, and finds out how one of the oldest working cinemas in the country has been saved from redevelopment(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The Akiki family, with its biodynamic fruit and vegetables and cheese-making clan the Spierings both hope to sell out of home-grown produce at one of London's oldest farmers' markets in Marylebone. A good day's trade will help the farms survive so every customer counts. Narrated by Denis Lawson(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Louise Gostelow and David Harper charge on the wily, windy moors of West Yorkshire, questing for antiques for their final auction. Louise gets her hands on a very early labour-saving device, as well as an illuminating bit of retro chic. David takes a shine to some theatre seats, but things get complicated when he tries to work out how many he can afford(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic swimming silver medallist Siobhan-Marie O'Connor, England cricketer Jake Ball, Great Britain Fed Cup tennis captain Anne Keothavong and former Wales prop Adam Jones join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon takes Kathy's keys and sneaks into Phil's house - only to be caught red-handed - and Mick worries when Linda is nowhere to be found, unaware she has taken Ollie out of school for the day. Suki settles into Walford, but it soon becomes clear she has an ulterior motive and will go to any lengths to reunite her family. Keegan has a new lease of life and throws himself into work, while Whitney makes a horrifying discovery(n)

programme.title: The Goes Wrong Show
programme.desc: Amateur dramatics group the Cornley Polytechnic Drama Society stages a family saga of lust and betrayal set in the American Deep South. A designer error means that one of the sets has been built at a literal 90 degrees to the vertical. Too expensive to re-stage, the gang decides to turn the cameras on their side and pretend that everything's normal. Gravity, however, has other ideas, and even getting in and out of chairs proves potentially limb-threatening. Comedy, starring Henry Lewis, Jonathan Sayer and Henry Shields(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Jo Brand, Simon Day, Kiri Pritchard-McClean and Henning Wehn join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies(n)

programme.title: King Gary
programme.desc: Knowing that one objection from a neighbour could thwart his plans to build an extension, Gary goes on a full charm-offensive by hosting a 'civilised' dinner party. Denise, an experienced soiree host, tries to take over the proceedings, while Big Gary is only interested in watching the boxing on Gary's new curve television alongside his gatecrashing golfing mates. Meanwhile, Terri uses the party as an opportunity to showcase her new mobile cocktail business venture. Comedy, with Tom Davis and Laura Checkley(n)

programme.title: BBC News Special
programme.desc: Huw Edwards introduces coverage of the moment the United Kingdom leaves the European Union. The departure was made possible after Prime Minister Boris Johnson's Brexit deal was backed by MPs in the wake of the general election. From February, the country enters an 11-month transition period to prepare for its new relationship with the EU(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Beth Hood wants to start a new life for her family down under, but her husband Ben is not convinced, so they decide to spend a trial week living in Perth, Australia(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Staff fear for their lives when an armed man bursts into a shop, and a family business finds itself under attack by a former employee demanding money(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
