programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Medical drama series set in a busy Midlands surgery. This time, a missing phone leads Bear down a miserable rabbit hole(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Tensions rise to deadly levels when a performance troupe of anarchists arrive in Kembleford, leaving Mrs McCarthy unimpressed and Bunty inspired(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor is in Cornwall, on hand to help a couple from London make their first joint property purchase with a budget of £750,000. Margherita also makes time to help out the head gardener at Trebah, one of the Great Gardens of Cornwall(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two dedicated farming families hope to impress potential buyers at The Scottish Game Fair with farm produced potato vodka and a selection of wild game burgers(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Louise Gostelow and David Harper scour Lancashire and Cumbria for items to sell at auction in Runcorn. On the way, Louise learns about the method for making Lancashire Cheese(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Matt Baker and Alex Jones(n)

programme.title: Easy Ways to Live Well
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and Steph McGovern challenge staff at a garden centre in the Wirral to test remedies for their aches and pains. The pair also tackle their own health concerns, including Steph's apprehension at exercising while pregnant and Hugh's worry that he's starting to lose his mental sharpness. Hugh then meets a family in Devon struggling with a very common ailment - fussy eating. With mealtimes a battlefield, he wants to discover if a counterintuitive idea may finally bring peace to the dinner table(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Natural history documentary in which spy cameras camouflaged as various creatures go 'undercover' in the animal world, getting up close and personal to film their behaviour. This time, cameras follow Japanese macaques in the snow-covered mountains of Japan, as they gather at steaming hot thermal pools to bathe and socialise. Elsewhere in the cool mountains of Mexico, cameras capture a spectacular gathering of billions of monarch butterflies, and in Bavaria, Germany, Spy Beaver gains unique access to the secretive world of the beaver(n)

programme.title: The Top Table
programme.desc: Young people come to the Top Table to face down our politicians in an exciting audience debate show, presented by Stephen Nolan(n)

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Former couples are reunited to talk about their break-ups. Chantelle wants Tevin to forgive her for sleeping with his best friend, and Rachel tries to win back Niraj(n)

programme.title: My Mate's a Bad Date
programme.desc: Oloni helps Dami, a 24-year-old with a history of dating failures, trying to help him own up to his disastrous habits(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crime - Are We Tough Enough?
programme.desc: Lawyers Chris Daw and Ayesha Nayyar spend time with different police forces, looking at how they are dealing with crime(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development of a three-bedroom semi in Darwen in Lancashire, a plot of land in Cromford in Derbyshire and an end terrace in Chatham in Kent(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Roisins sample life in Brisbane for a week, but while Julia's dream is to move to Australia in the hope of a better lifestyle for their children, husband Hussein is not so sure(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A shop owner grapples with a dangerous gunman as his two daughters watch in horror, and a frail 93-year-old has money stolen, but a large number of visitors could be the culprit(n)

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ruhma feels out of her depth with a deaf patient, Karen uncovers a patient's racy pastime and Zara upsets Ayesha's enthusiasm for her dissertation(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The Kembleford Ramblers' annual outing takes a tragic turn when the local lord is found strangled, so the cleric gets his walking boots on to solve the crime. Murder mystery, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a rural property shopper who has £250,000 to buy her ideal country home in north Wales and has drafted in her best friend for support. Along the way, the host takes a historic train ride along the beautiful coastline and meets the volunteer train driver who is helping keep Wales's railway heritage on track(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Three farm managers, all critical to the running of their businesses, attend an agricultural awards ceremony in London, where they battle it out for Farm Manager of the Year(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Louise Gostelow and David Harper go antique shopping in Wales, where Louise adds to her ever-growing collection of chairs and David takes a risk with items he knows nothing about(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Without alcohol, Linda suggests a day out with Ollie and Mick to distract herself, but her good mood is short lived. Kathy checks on Bobby but tensions soon escalate between her and Sharon, forcing Bobby to intervene. Iqra tries to reason with Keegan, while Ash and Suki begin to build bridges(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Mary Berry gives the contestants just one hour and 45 minutes to create their ultimate curry night, including two different dishes, a traditional bread and a fried snack. This is followed by Chris Bavin's challenge to rustle something up using an ingredient that features on most people's weekly shopping list, and the cook-off between this week's weakest two contestants. Claudia Winkleman hosts(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Christopher Williams' body is found on a boat out at sea. All evidence points towards a deadly fall, but Jack is bothered by the victim's strange appearance and the fact that his car was abandoned miles out into the woods. Suspicion fall on his wife and friends - but with everyone having an alibi, the police drift further away from the truth. Meanwhile, Jack's daughter Siobhan visits and encourages him to accept Anna's offer of joining her on her travels, leaving the DI with a big decision to make(n)

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents a review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Buxton, Derbyshire, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

How will they rank on the leaderboard? programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on the royals' rollercoaster relationship with the press, beginning with the Queen's 1953-1954 Commonwealth tour, when cameramen worked hand-in-hand with the palace. A few years later, newspaper owner Lord Altrincham was attacked for daring to publish criticisms of the Queen, but by the mid-1970s Princess Margaret's hedonistic lifestyle was the subject of increasing press attention. But that was only the beginning. In the 1990s, the press has a field day over the breakdown of Charles and Diana's marriage, feeding on royal scandals(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Will and Ellie go on their first date with a trip to the flicks. However, a scream from the projection booth cuts the romance short as they discover a brutal murder has taken place within the cinema. Geordie arrives to investigate and soon they've found movie star connections, oddball employees and a link to the Asian subcontinent, as a shadowy figure emerges from Jack Chapman's past and refuses to retreat without causing a stir. Cathy's mother takes the children out for the day, which ends up worrying Cathy far more than it should. Robson Green, Tom Brittney, Lauren Carse and Kacey Ainsworth star(n)

programme.title: The Greek Islands with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Known as the Paradise Islands, the Sporades' lush pine forests and coastlines are simply breathtaking. On Skiathos, Julia boards a fishing boat in search of a secret world of hidden coves and deserted beaches. Heading inland, she visits the hillside Monastery of Evangelistria, one of Greece's most important religious sites, before island-hopping to neighbouring Skopelos for a taste of marriage, Greek style. After trying on the island's traditional wedding dress, her trip ends at the tiny Chapel of St John(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: After waking from a nap, Daniel confuses Bethany for Sinead, and kisses her. Geoff continues to spread lies about Yasmeen's drinking, while Steve and Tracy are railroaded into attending Ken's book club as part of their relationship-building exercise(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: When a young man is found dead and stark naked out on the Fens, Will and Geordie discover ties to a Cambridge research project experimenting with mind-altering drugs. Its arrogant professor is a proponent of the drug LSD in his psychotherapy sessions, but Will and Geordie start wondering whether there is more going on. As the case proceeds, Will's feelings of guilt about his father's death and his conflicted feelings about Ellie start to take their toll. Elsewhere, the Keatings suffer an awkward dinner party and the Chapman feud shows no sign of relenting. Robson Green and Tom Brittney star(n)

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: Tom Bradby is joined by special guests in the studio, and ITV News presenters and correspondents around the country, for the countdown to the historic moment the UK leaves the European Union. Tonight's event comes after three extensions and three-and-a-half years following the referendum on the issue of continued membership. Plus, the day's other national and international headlines.(n)

programme.title: Cold Feet

programme.desc: Sian Williams and Ranj Singh present the consumer health show. How will they rank on the leaderboard? programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on the royals' rollercoaster relationship with the press, beginning with the Queen's 1953-1954 Commonwealth tour, when cameramen worked hand-in-hand with the palace. A few years later, newspaper owner Lord Altrincham was attacked for daring to publish criticisms of the Queen, but by the mid-1970s Princess Margaret's hedonistic lifestyle was the subject of increasing press attention. But that was only the beginning. In the 1990s, the press has a field day over the breakdown of Charles and Diana's marriage, feeding on royal scandals(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Will and Ellie go on their first date with a trip to the flicks. However, a scream from the projection booth cuts the romance short as they discover a brutal murder has taken place within the cinema. Geordie arrives to investigate and soon they've found movie star connections, oddball employees and a link to the Asian subcontinent, as a shadowy figure emerges from Jack Chapman's past and refuses to retreat without causing a stir. Cathy's mother takes the children out for the day, which ends up worrying Cathy far more than it should. Robson Green, Tom Brittney, Lauren Carse and Kacey Ainsworth star(n)

programme.title: The Greek Islands with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Known as the Paradise Islands, the Sporades' lush pine forests and coastlines are simply breathtaking. On Skiathos, Julia boards a fishing boat in search of a secret world of hidden coves and deserted beaches. Heading inland, she visits the hillside Monastery of Evangelistria, one of Greece's most important religious sites, before island-hopping to neighbouring Skopelos for a taste of marriage, Greek style. After trying on the island's traditional wedding dress, her trip ends at the tiny Chapel of St John(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: When a young man is found dead and stark naked out on the Fens, Will and Geordie discover ties to a Cambridge research project experimenting with mind-altering drugs. Its arrogant professor is a proponent of the drug LSD in his psychotherapy sessions, but Will and Geordie start wondering whether there is more going on. As the case proceeds, Will's feelings of guilt about his father's death and his conflicted feelings about Ellie start to take their toll. programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. Called in to investigate, Morse finds a coded letter in her bag and sets about trying to decipher it, gradually unravelling a tangled web of hidden passion and corruption at the insurance office where she worked. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey(n)

Margo notices his bad mood and takes steps to save the situation, while Louisa worries about Spiros, who has locked himself in a room to sing sad love songs(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police are called in to investigate when a local judge is the target of a series of mysterious attacks, but the case takes a sinister turn when the prime suspect is found dead on his own doorstep. programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When an unpopular employee at a military psychiatric hospital is found murdered, there is no shortage of suspects, but a colleague of the man, an exiled Polish Jew, holds himself responsible for the death. Foyle is convinced there is another explanation, but his priorities shift when news of a missing boy from London reaches Hastings. Starring Michael Kitchen, Phyllida Law and Nicholas Woodeson(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth falls victim to depression after failing to prevent the murder of a society girl by notorious art thief Marrascaud. His confidence shattered, Poirot eventually returns to work when a lonely chauffeur begs him to find his missing soulmate, the maid of a famous Russian dancer. The investigation takes him to the Swiss Alps, where, against all odds, he stumbles upon a hotel thought to be Marrascaud's hideout - leading to a very personal showdown. The penultimate episode of the long-running detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Simon Callow, Orla Brady, Patrick Ryecart, Nigel Lindsay, Morven Christie, Tom Austen and Rupert Evans(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A man is found shot dead in a hotel room and the cash he was carrying has gone missing. Ronnie and Sam discover the victim owed his ex-wife thousands of pounds in child support for their terminally ill son and had been lying to his girlfriend about his line of work. As the investigation progresses, the detectives focus their inquiries on the child's grandfather. Did he commit murder to recoup the unpaid money or is there more to this family set-up than first appears? Meanwhile, at the Crown Prosecution Service, Kate and Jake work out their differences. Guest starring Ian Bleasdale (Casualty)(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An aristocrat goes missing, along with his collection of explicit erotic paintings - and Morse's investigation provides him with a close-up view of the upper crust's dark side. What is more, there is a further culture shock lying in wait - the prospect of working with a new female pathologist. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Amanda Hillwood, Patricia Hodge and Clifford Rose(n)

programme.desc: The Tates fight for a life on the roadside while an idea is fermenting for Turner. Rachel has a suggestion for Christopher, and Luke's fate continues to hang in the balance(n)

Peggy is delighted when David's new radio starts picking up police broadcasts, but a news reporter doesn't share her enthusiasm. Rural drama, with Gwen Taylor and David Lonsdale(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Gloomy new tenant Mr Gray moves into Rigsby's boarding house, but proves so depressed that he barely seems to notice the dingy surroundings, and keeps talking about 'going to a better place' - raising fears he is contemplating suicide. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter and David Swift(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Tired of getting nowhere with Miss Jones, Rigsby switches his amorous attentions to new lodger Marilyn, who turns out to be an exotic dancer - complete with python. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Robin find a rare plant in the Ropers' garden. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An aristocrat goes missing, along with his collection of explicit erotic paintings - and Morse's investigation provides him with a close-up view of the upper crust's dark side. What is more, there is a further culture shock lying in wait - the prospect of working with a new female pathologist. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Amanda Hillwood, Patricia Hodge and Clifford Rose(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The rural drama returns after a winter break. A woman receives a shock when a man claiming to be her dead husband surfaces in the village and attempts to pick up the threads of his former life - though the locals aren't convinced of his identity. Lord Ashfordly's behaviour takes a strange turn after he eats some of Peggy's peculiar batch of mushrooms. Gwen Taylor and Rupert Vansittart star(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Pollard has an expensive gift for Mrs Bates. Nick has plans to make Seth a radio star. Luke returns home to find his problems are only just beginning(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned is in charge of the farm when disaster strikes, while Luke faces up to the revenge of the Dingle family. Viv tries to put off the day of reckoning with Alan Turner(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Samir tells Deirdre he does not want her supporting him. Bet goes to Croydon to find Raquel. Martin tells Gail he is sick of Nick, and Curly wants Andy to move out(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly tries his best to break Paula and Andy up at work, Samir tells Deirdre he is unhappy living off her and wants to find work. Tanya is stunned to see Raquel back(n)

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Henry Miller comes to stay with the family, and stirs up tension among Louisa and her children with his strange behaviour and the controversial content of his books. Sven is arrested after the government announces a crackdown on what it considers 'immoral behaviour', and Larry rushes to his aid, acquiring books on fascism and miscarriages of justice from the countess to help him take matters into his own hands. Spiros continues to pine for his wife, and Basil makes a surprise appearance on the island, leaving the family on edge about Aunt Hermione's estate(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The rural drama returns after a winter break. A woman receives a shock when a man claiming to be her dead husband surfaces in the village and attempts to pick up the threads of his former life - though the locals aren't convinced of his identity. Lord Ashfordly's behaviour takes a strange turn after he eats some of Peggy's peculiar batch of mushrooms. Gwen Taylor and Rupert Vansittart star(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle is enlisted as the police representative on the Victory Day Celebrations Committee, alongside his old friend John Kiefer representing the American forces stationed at Hastings. But a murder of one of their number puts paid to the preparations when evidence points to the guilt of another member, and investigations uncover truths the Allies would rather remain hidden. Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks star, with guest Jay Benedict(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis struggle to determine whether a death during a college-based clinical trial for a new antidepressant was murder or suicide, and receive little assistance from the institution's evasive psychiatric professor. A second student's demise then leads to the revelation that the trial's participants are all operating under the influence of a mind-altering drug, and that the lines between love, obsession and madness have become dangerously blurred. Guest starring Douglas Henshall (Primeval), Lucy Liemann (Rev) and Thomas Brodie Sangster (Love Actually)(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Ronnie and Sam lead the investigation into the death of a wealthy woman whose body was dumped in the Thames. The team initially focuses inquiries on the victim's abusive ex-husband, with whom she had been involved in a bitter struggle over custody of their daughter Holly, but the case heads in a different direction when the detectives discover the teenager was infatuated with her mother's new partner. With Patrick Baladi and an appearance by Bradley Walsh's son Barney(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An aristocrat goes missing, along with his collection of explicit erotic paintings - and Morse's investigation provides him with a close-up view of the upper crust's dark side. What is more, there is a further culture shock lying in wait - the prospect of working with a new female pathologist. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Amanda Hillwood, Patricia Hodge and Clifford Rose(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The master of a prestigious college asks Morse to investigate the disappearance of one of his colleagues. The subsequent discovery of a dismembered torso soon has everyone fearing the worst - until the missing man turns up alive and well. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, James Grout, Beatie Edney, Sian Thomas and Barry Foster(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Alan starts dating a posh new girlfriend, but makes the mistake of asking Rigsby for advice on how to conduct himself in polite company - a sure-fire recipe for disaster. Comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: New tenant Osborne seemingly dies after eating a bowl of Miss Jones' home-made soup, plunging the household into mourning - but Rigsby is the one who ends up being measured for a coffin. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin's plans for a romantic dinner for two are ruined when the Ropers have an argument. Seventies comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The master of a prestigious college asks Morse to investigate the disappearance of one of his colleagues. The subsequent discovery of a dismembered torso soon has everyone fearing the worst - until the missing man turns up alive and well. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, James Grout, Beatie Edney, Sian Thomas and Barry Foster(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Lord Ashfordly's niece comes to stay in Aidensfield following the breakdown of her marriage, but immediately suffers another blow when her prize racehorse is kidnapped and held to ransom. Romance is in the air for Rosie as a biker gang turns up in town, and a seedy photographer promises to make a star of Phil Bellamy's stepdaughter Jane. Rural drama, starring Vanessa Hehir and Lauren Drummond(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Betty is counting the cost after her pop concert. Bernard's suggestion leaves his family aghast. Frank returns from hospital with something to say to Kim(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank struggles with the quiet life, and Seth fights back against enforced retirement. On the day of the Post Office trial, Alan's world falls apart when he hears the evidence(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Andy is put out when Curly coldly accepts his resignation. Mike is horrified when Steve rents the flat next to him. Samir gets a job, working at a Moroccan restaurant(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Bet is furious with Charlie for telling Tanya about Raquel's misfortune. Curly cooks Raquel a meal and tries to buck her up. Samir starts work at the Casablanca restaurant(n)

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: The family meets members of a travelling circus, and gets involved in helping them to drum up business. Larry falls in love with a contortionist, while Margo takes to the ring in person to perform a very dangerous act. Gerry is more concerned with making sure all of the animals featured in the show are well-treated. Spiros cannot bring himself to return to his empty house and begins spending more and more time at the Durrells' home - but the magic of the circus brings him and Louisa closer together(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Lord Ashfordly's niece comes to stay in Aidensfield following the breakdown of her marriage, but immediately suffers another blow when her prize racehorse is kidnapped and held to ransom. Romance is in the air for Rosie as a biker gang turns up in town, and a seedy photographer promises to make a star of Phil Bellamy's stepdaughter Jane. Rural drama, starring Vanessa Hehir and Lauren Drummond(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A young woman asks Poirot to investigate her father's murder - a 14-year-old crime for which her mother was found guilty and executed. The detective agrees to investigate, but warns his client that he may discover things she does not want to hear. David Suchet stars, with Rachael Stirling, Aidan Gillen, Aimee Mullens, Toby Stephens, Gemma Jones, Patrick Malahide and Annette Badland(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa is not best pleased when her new art teacher Erica wants to try out some rather alternative ideas on the children, while Dr Timoney is involved in a car accident outside the school. Penhale is confused when he is offered a transfer to Exeter, Bert decides to go back to being a handyman, and Louisa and Martin are intrigued when Dr Timoney invites them for an additional therapy session. Plus, an American tourist seeks help from the GP. Comedy drama, with a cameo appearance by Sigourney Weaver(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa worries Dr Timoney's suggestion that she and Martin aren't meant to be together might have prompted him to leave Portwenn for good. Calling on Ruth, Morwenna and Janice for help, she tries to track the down the missing GP, with no luck. However, what Dr Ellingham's friends and family fail to realise is that he is actually trapped up at the Winton farm, where desperate wife Annie is trying to coerce him into performing a life-saving operation on her terminally ill husband Jim. Meanwhile, Ruth and Bert seek out Al to offer him a proposition regarding the whisky and the B&B. Medical drama, starring Martin Clunes(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: An elderly woman is found dead in her flat after a break-in, leading Ronnie and Sam to suspect a burglary gone wrong. However, following a tip-off from the victim's grand-daughter Connie, the detectives' attention turns to live-in carer Cecile, who is promptly arrested. But the accused points the finger back at Connie, claiming she had been deliberately starving her grandmother. Who will the team believe? With Jan Francis, Daniel Casey and Ramon Tikaram(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The master of a prestigious college asks Morse to investigate the disappearance of one of his colleagues. The subsequent discovery of a dismembered torso soon has everyone fearing the worst - until the missing man turns up alive and well. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, James Grout, Beatie Edney, Sian Thomas and Barry Foster(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tanya is stunned when Des tells her Alex has settled out of court, and Barnes warns Bet that Tanya is nothing but trouble. The McDonalds entertain Deirdre and Samir(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly is fed up when Andy receives a glowing management assessment, Jack loses his electricity money on the horses, and Bet attempts to track down Raquel(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The McAllisters close ranks to support their son. Pollard and Betty make an unlikely partnership, while Zoe has some good news for Linda Glover(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Heritage Farm hosts a summer fair, while Betty's rock concert pulls in the crowds. Seth and Samson save the day for the cricket team, but there is an accident on the way back(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A runaway heiress accompanies her crooked boyfriend on a series of armed robberies. But when the Sixties version of Bonnie and Clyde get into trouble they kidnap Helen, who attempts to persuade the teenager to ditch her partner in crime. Meanwhile, Peggy suggests David should take over the funeral parlour while Bernie is away. Rural drama with Sophie Ward, David Stockwell and Gwen Taylor(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Miss Jones falls for new guest Hilary, a budding playwright who sends Rigsby into fits of jealousy by offering her the starring role in his latest masterpiece. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter and guest Peter Bowles(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby splashes out on a new sports car in the hope of impressing Miss Jones, but a cruel practical joke by Philip convinces him he has run over his feline friend Vienna. Leonard Rossiter and Frances de la Tour star(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Mr Roper decides he will not allow the flatmates to have a party. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. Called in to investigate, Morse finds a coded letter in her bag and sets about trying to decipher it, gradually unravelling a tangled web of hidden passion and corruption at the insurance office where she worked. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police are called in to investigate when a local judge is the target of a series of mysterious attacks, but the case takes a sinister turn when the prime suspect is found dead on his own doorstep. Peggy is delighted when David's new radio starts picking up police broadcasts, but a news reporter doesn't share her enthusiasm. Rural drama, with Gwen Taylor and David Lonsdale(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Tates fight for a life on the roadside while an idea is fermenting for Turner. Rachel has a suggestion for Christopher, and Luke's fate continues to hang in the balance(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel plans to get Chris out and about, Frank's illness throws a shadow over his future with Kim, Luke continues to languish behind bars, and Pollard plans a big shock for Betty(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly asks Paula round for a meal, Bet tracks Raquel down to a pub where she has been working, while Liz and Jim leave for a holiday in Ireland(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly is horrified to see Paula and Andy leaving number 11 together in the morning. Deirdre is delighted when Samir lets his plane leave without him(n)

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: The family plans a party to celebrate Gerry turning 13, but the occasion is disrupted by a swarm of mosquitoes and the birthday boy's frustrations at his mother and siblings not realising that he is growing up. Margo notices his bad mood and takes steps to save the situation, while Louisa worries about Spiros, who has locked himself in a room to sing sad love songs(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police are called in to investigate when a local judge is the target of a series of mysterious attacks, but the case takes a sinister turn when the prime suspect is found dead on his own doorstep. Peggy is delighted when David's new radio starts picking up police broadcasts, but a news reporter doesn't share her enthusiasm. Rural drama, with Gwen Taylor and David Lonsdale(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When an unpopular employee at a military psychiatric hospital is found murdered, there is no shortage of suspects, but a colleague of the man, an exiled Polish Jew, holds himself responsible for the death. Foyle is convinced there is another explanation, but his priorities shift when news of a missing boy from London reaches Hastings. Starring Michael Kitchen, Phyllida Law and Nicholas Woodeson(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth falls victim to depression after failing to prevent the murder of a society girl by notorious art thief Marrascaud. His confidence shattered, Poirot eventually returns to work when a lonely chauffeur begs him to find his missing soulmate, the maid of a famous Russian dancer. The investigation takes him to the Swiss Alps, where, against all odds, he stumbles upon a hotel thought to be Marrascaud's hideout - leading to a very personal showdown. The penultimate episode of the long-running detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Simon Callow, Orla Brady, Patrick Ryecart, Nigel Lindsay, Morven Christie, Tom Austen and Rupert Evans(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A man is found shot dead in a hotel room and the cash he was carrying has gone missing. Ronnie and Sam discover the victim owed his ex-wife thousands of pounds in child support for their terminally ill son and had been lying to his girlfriend about his line of work. As the investigation progresses, the detectives focus their inquiries on the child's grandfather. Did he commit murder to recoup the unpaid money or is there more to this family set-up than first appears? Meanwhile, at the Crown Prosecution Service, Kate and Jake work out their differences. Guest starring Ian Bleasdale (Casualty)(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. Called in to investigate, Morse finds a coded letter in her bag and sets about trying to decipher it, gradually unravelling a tangled web of hidden passion and corruption at the insurance office where she worked. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Betty is counting the cost after her pop concert. Bernard's suggestion leaves his family aghast. Frank returns from hospital with something to say to Kim(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank struggles with the quiet life, and Seth fights back against enforced retirement. On the day of the Post Office trial, Alan's world falls apart when he hears the evidence(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Andy is put out when Curly coldly accepts his resignation. Mike is horrified when Steve rents the flat next to him. Samir gets a job, working at a Moroccan restaurant(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Bet is furious with Charlie for telling Tanya about Raquel's misfortune. Curly cooks Raquel a meal and tries to buck her up. Samir starts work at the Casablanca restaurant(n)

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: The family meets members of a travelling circus, and gets involved in helping them to drum up business. Larry falls in love with a contortionist, while Margo takes to the ring in person to perform a very dangerous act. Gerry is more concerned with making sure all of the animals featured in the show are well-treated. Spiros cannot bring himself to return to his empty house and begins spending more and more time at the Durrells' home - but the magic of the circus brings him and Louisa closer together(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Lord Ashfordly's niece comes to stay in Aidensfield following the breakdown of her marriage, but immediately suffers another blow when her prize racehorse is kidnapped and held to ransom. Romance is in the air for Rosie as a biker gang turns up in town, and a seedy photographer promises to make a star of Phil Bellamy's stepdaughter Jane. Rural drama, starring Vanessa Hehir and Lauren Drummond(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A young woman asks Poirot to investigate her father's murder - a 14-year-old crime for which her mother was found guilty and executed. The detective agrees to investigate, but warns his client that he may discover things she does not want to hear. David Suchet stars, with Rachael Stirling, Aidan Gillen, Aimee Mullens, Toby Stephens, Gemma Jones, Patrick Malahide and Annette Badland(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa is not best pleased when her new art teacher Erica wants to try out some rather alternative ideas on the children, while Dr Timoney is involved in a car accident outside the school. Penhale is confused when he is offered a transfer to Exeter, Bert decides to go back to being a handyman, and Louisa and Martin are intrigued when Dr Timoney invites them for an additional therapy session. Plus, an American tourist seeks help from the GP. Comedy drama, with a cameo appearance by Sigourney Weaver(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa worries Dr Timoney's suggestion that she and Martin aren't meant to be together might have prompted him to leave Portwenn for good. Calling on Ruth, Morwenna and Janice for help, she tries to track the down the missing GP, with no luck. However, what Dr Ellingham's friends and family fail to realise is that he is actually trapped up at the Winton farm, where desperate wife Annie is trying to coerce him into performing a life-saving operation on her terminally ill husband Jim. Meanwhile, Ruth and Bert seek out Al to offer him a proposition regarding the whisky and the B&B. Medical drama, starring Martin Clunes(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: An elderly woman is found dead in her flat after a break-in, leading Ronnie and Sam to suspect a burglary gone wrong. However, following a tip-off from the victim's grand-daughter Connie, the detectives' attention turns to live-in carer Cecile, who is promptly arrested. But the accused points the finger back at Connie, claiming she had been deliberately starving her grandmother. Who will the team believe? With Jan Francis, Daniel Casey and Ramon Tikaram(n)

programme.title: Goals of the 90s

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: Quincy stumbles upon a plot devised by a group of Nazi war criminals to use an Egyptian mummy to smuggle rare gems from Cairo to Los Angeles(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Excavations at a demolition site uncover a human skeleton encased in concrete, which leads to the reopening on an unsolved murder case from 18 years previously(n)

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: An intruder begins breaking into people's homes and destroying their plaster statuettes of Napoleon, but leaving more valuable items untouched. Holmes resolves to find out why, eventually uncovering a twisted tale of theft and murder. Jeremy Brett stars as the master detective(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur, Ray and Dave go on a day-trip to Calais, intending to stock the Winchester's cellars with duty-free plonk, but soon find themselves in trouble. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle investigate the death of a burglar who was killed after seeing classified information about a foreign country, and resort to theft in a bid to uncover the embassy's sinister secrets. Liz Fraser and Michael Elphick guest star(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A bad dream has Regan's girlfriend fearing for his safety, and her visions ring true when he gets involved in a case involving a stolen lorryload of cigars. The plot thickens when illicit cocaine is retrieved and arms stolen from the US are discovered to be on their way to a military faction in Ireland. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The coroner is hired as technical adviser on a movie inspired by a famous homicide case, and becomes convinced the real murderer is still at large(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A gang of art thieves is arrested, but the priceless drawings they stole are still missing. Theo is forced to work with a world famous criminologist to recover the missing artwork(n)

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Suffering from nervous exhaustion, Holmes sets off for a break on the wild Cornish peninsula, where he hopes the peace and quiet will help him convalesce. Unfortunately, several murders ensure his holiday is far from relaxing. Period detective drama, guest starring Denis Quilley(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur attempts to protect his friend Sidney, who on mistakenly being diagnosed with a fatal illness, arranged for a hitman to kill him so his wife would benefit from his life insurance. While Ray attempts to counter the deal, the best safe house the dodgy dealer can provide is a leaky caravan in Brighton, the property of Dave's sister. Andrew Sachs guest stars, with George Cole and Gary Webster(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: The resources of CI5 are stretched when a mentally unbalanced former soldier starts killing hospital staff, believing them responsible for the death of his wife and for the brain damage suffered by his young daughter. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A former criminal and friend of Regan's wins a large amount of money on the pools - but then receives blackmailing phone calls regarding a crime from 11 years earlier for which he was never caught. Hard-hitting drama, guest starring Edward Judd, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Heptathlete Louise Hazel, comedian Joel Dommett, 1980s music star Sinitta and actor Les Dennis team up to take part in the quiz show, in which they must answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit - all without being caught out by the 'quiz genius' Chaser. Presented by Bradley Walsh(n)

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove try to break into the classic tractor market, as they continue their quest to make money out of buying and selling people's unwanted automotive junk(n)

programme.title: Billy Connolly's Great American Trail
programme.desc: Billy's final leg begins in Virginia on the trail of bootleggers dealing in moonshine before he hears the story of the amazing woman who led hundreds of slaves to their freedom. He stops off at a farm where he encounters bizarre heads of all the US presidents, learns about the opioid crisis - the biggest epidemic in American history - and visits the backroom of flamboyant designer Manuel Cuuevas, who was responsible for dressing rock stars including Elvis, Cher and the Beatles. The comedian's tour finally ends in Nashville at the home of country music, the Grand Ole Opry(n)

programme.title: Waterworld (1995)
programme.desc: In the future, environmental disasters have left the planet flooded. A web-footed mutant mariner helps a woman and an orphan girl search for a mythical haven of dry land, with the help of a map tattooed on the child's back - but in the process has to fend off a villainous crew of nicotine-addicted pirates. Sci-fi adventure, starring Kevin Costner, Dennis Hopper, Jeanne Tripplehorn, Tina Majorino and Rick Aviles(n)

programme.title: Darts: The Masters Live
programme.desc: Jacqui Oatley presents coverage of day one from Arena MK in Milton Keynes, where the opening first-round contests get under way. programme.title: Billy Connolly's Great American Trail
programme.desc: Billy's final leg begins in Virginia on the trail of bootleggers dealing in moonshine before he hears the story of the amazing woman who led hundreds of slaves to their freedom. He stops off at a farm where he encounters bizarre heads of all the US presidents, learns about the opioid crisis - the biggest epidemic in American history - and visits the backroom of flamboyant designer Manuel Cuuevas, who was responsible for dressing rock stars including Elvis, Cher and the Beatles. The comedian's tour finally ends in Nashville at the home of country music, the Grand Ole Opry(n)

programme.title: Waterworld (1995)
programme.desc: In the future, environmental disasters have left the planet flooded. A web-footed mutant mariner helps a woman and an orphan girl search for a mythical haven of dry land, with the help of a map tattooed on the child's back - but in the process has to fend off a villainous crew of nicotine-addicted pirates. Sci-fi adventure, starring Kevin Costner, Dennis Hopper, Jeanne Tripplehorn, Tina Majorino and Rick Aviles(n)

programme.title: Farage: The Man Who Made Brexit
programme.desc: Filmed over five months, this documentary explores the world of the British politician Nigel Farage as he attempts to build on his historic success in May 2019's European elections. This documentary captures the inside story of Farage's doomed attempt to persuade Boris Johnson to join a Leavers' Alliance with him, and also charts Farage's influence in changing British politics in the wake of the 2016 EU referendum. With racism returning to haunt the party and plummeting poll ratings, is December 2019's general election the event that finally persuades Farage that the UK will never buy what he is selling? Or can he claim victory with a brand-new right-leaning Tory government that supports much of what he stands for?(n)

programme.title: Auschwitz Untold: In Colour
programme.desc: A powerful and revelatory account of one of the most hideous crimes in human history told from the perspectives of 16 extraordinary Holocaust survivors. The survivors retell of the rise of fascism, the uprooting of families, and the long journey from centuries-old Jewish communities into Auschwitz. The film includes a rare and haunting collection of photographs called the 'Auschwitz Album', and chronicles with unflinching detail the survival challenges at Auschwitz-Birkenau, as well as tales of resistance inside the camp, including sabotaging armaments for the Nazi war machine(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: This edition focuses on the importance of the father-son relationship and the potential consequences and difficulties when this relationship is not a positive one. Frontline officers talk frankly about their experiences of how the breakdown of this pivotal formative experience can lead to young men perpetuating cycles of violence, lacking confidence, or struggling with mental health problems(n)

programme.title: Bring Back the Bush: Where Did Our Pubic Hair Go?
programme.desc: Author Chidera Eggerue explores people's relationship with pubic hair, investigating why so many women remove their pubic hair when even a few decades ago it wasn't seen as necessary. Along the way, she challenges herself and her peers to grow out their pubic hair and put on an exhibition in which they reveal their naked, natural bodies to the world(n)

programme.title: First Dates

Other participants are Val, 78, who meets 82-year-old ex-welder Roy, and singleton Del, 36. Her date is musician Michael, who has let his work get in the way of love until now(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

An 82-year-old patient has difficulty breathing, and doctors suspect he has developed life-threatening sepsis and need to act fast to try to halt the spread of infection(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick and his team treat Staffordshire bull terrier Lulu, who has been hit by a car and had her pelvis smashed into more than 25 pieces. The professor's only option is to pin the larger pieces back together in an operation that will require all his expertise. Noel and his team also treat a giant schnauzer called Florence who has a sore knee and a pointy-eared Jack Russell called Muffin who has a mysterious problem with her back legs(n)

programme.title: Britain's Parking Hell
programme.desc: In the Pembrokeshire town of Tenby, Welsh and English rugby fans seem more focused on kick-off time than parking, threatening to turn its roads into a ruck of cars. Meanwhile in Cardiff, a city with the dubious title of Britain's third-worst place to park, two carloads of parking desperadoes search for a space on match day. In Ivybridge, Devon, locals claim a ticket machine with a typo problem has resulted in an avalanche of unjust tickets and are determined to take their fight to the top(n)

programme.title: Red Arrows Take America
programme.desc: The display team fly over Niagara Falls before arriving in Toronto, where they demonstrate their aerobatic moves alongside their Canadian Air Force counterparts, the Snowbirds. The pilots then meet astronaut Chris Hadfield, who is given the rare opportunity to fly in one of the team's jets. As their tour continues, the pilots perform at the Spirit of St Louis Air Show, before travelling north to Rocky Mountain Airport, where several of the jets suffer deflated tyres in the heat(n)

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the use of trains in transporting millions of prisoners to ghettos and death camps during the Holocaust. The programme retraces how the implementation of Nazi Germany's first anti-Jewish laws coincided with a drive to construct the world's most extensive rail network, and features contributions from survivors, including a Polish man who worked as a child slave in building the railway, who reveals how he narrowly escaped being transported to Auschwitz by hiding in a cemetery(n)

programme.title: Rich Kids Go Homeless
programme.desc: Rukaya, a youngster who lives in a palatial seven-bedroom house with her parents, agrees to sleep rough on the streets of London for three nights. She soon feels vulnerable on the streets when a group of men intimidate her and a fellow homeless woman. Begging brings only 20p, so the second night is spent in a shelter, where she meets a woman whose alcoholism cost her her family(n)

programme.title: 5 News Tonight
programme.desc: A round-up of the evening's headlines, including coverage of the latest national and international stories(n)

programme.title: DHL: The World's Biggest Delivery Company
programme.desc: At one of DHL's major hubs inside East Midlands Airport, shipping companies have to deal with huge volumes of packages each day. The key is a process known as 'the sort', in which people load and unload a vast automated system of conveyor belts. In South Africa, cameras follow Hloni as he attempts to go global with his new Sowetan vodka brand. Narrated by Lewis MacLeod(n)

programme.title: Secret Scotland
programme.desc: Perthshire is known as Scotland's 'big tree country' and with its lush forest it's easy to see why. Susan goes behind the scenes with Lady Mansfield at the magnificent Scone Palace, the historical crowning site of Scotland's kings. She also explores the secrets of the Hermitage in Dunkeld, a purpose-built forest playground for the aristocracy. Then it's on to the Birnam Oak, said to have inspired the writing of Macbeth. There's a chance to learn the art of glassmaking as well as meet a colony of bees who create special honey from Scotland's heather. Susan ends her journey in the city of Dundee(n)

programme.title: Holidaying with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: The singer takes in Valletta, tiny capital of the Mediterranean island nation of Malta, by land and sea. Tommy Walsh enjoys an escorted tour of Spain, where he visits the Plaza de Espania in Seville and enjoys a horse and carriage ride round the centre. He also goes to Granada, where he visits the Alhambra and is treated to a flamenco show. Plus, Chris Bisson explores Northern Ireland's coastline, replete with waterfalls and kayaking opportunities. There's also time to absorb the centre of Belfast on a black cab tour of the city(n)

programme.title: Inside the World's Greatest Hotels
programme.desc: A look behind the scenes at the Dorchester in London's affluent area of Mayfair, which has been associated with the rich and famous since it first opened in 1931. A royal stag party is just one of the subjects covered in the programme, along with fascinating accounts of engagements, honeymoons and an untimely death(n)

programme.title: The Wonderful World of Chocolate
programme.desc: Food giant Nestlé opens the doors of its Perugia factory in Italy, where production manager Davide looks into the secrets of what is undeniably a labour of love. Chocolatier Prudence Staite has a big challenge ahead, creating a full-blown chocolate replica of a much-loved vintage motorbike for the National Motorcycle Museum in Solihull, as it celebrates its 35th birthday(n)

programme.title: The Naked Jungle (1954)
programme.desc: A tough South American cocoa plantation owner and his newly arrived mail-order bride are menaced by a huge column of ferocious killer ants destroying everything in their path. Thriller, starring Charlton Heston, Eleanor Parker and William Conrad(n)

programme.title: Two Thousand Women (1944)
programme.desc: Female prisoners struggle to cope with living in a German internment camp, but find common cause in making life difficult for their captors. When three British airmen are shot down over the prison, they keep them hidden from the Germans while plotting a way to smuggle them out safely. Second World War comedy, with Phyllis Calvert, Flora Robson, Patricia Roc and Renee Houston(n)

programme.title: 3:10 to Yuma (1957)
programme.desc: A farmer in need of money agrees to guard a notorious outlaw until the train arrives to transport him to jail. Meanwhile, the pair are holed up in a hotel, where his prisoner plays mind games with him - and the villain's cohorts are waiting outside ready to engineer an escape. Western adapted from a novel by Elmore Leonard, with Van Heflin, Glenn Ford, Felicia Farr and Leora Dana(n)

programme.title: The Enemy Below (1957)
programme.desc: The captain of an American destroyer in the South Atlantic is still recovering from the sinking of his previous vessel. He tries to outwit a cunning German U-boat commander, but comes to respect the courage of his enemy. However, his crew fears he is not up to the challenge of defeating his opponent. Second World War thriller, starring Robert Mitchum, Curt Jurgens, Theodore Bikel and David Hedison(n)

programme.title: Footloose (2011)
programme.desc: A teenager from Boston moves to a small conservative town, and discovers the council and a preacher have enforced a ban on loud music and dancing. He openly defies the harsh rules, revitalising the local youth and winning the heart of the reverend's daughter. Remake of the 1984 drama, with Kenny Wormald, Julianne Hough and Dennis Quaid(n)

programme.title: The Lighthouse Interview Special
programme.desc: Actors Willem Dafoe and Robert Pattinson talk about going to extremes in The Lighthouse, an eerie new drama from the director of The Witch, Robert Eggers(n)

programme.title: Taken 2 (2012)
programme.desc: Former CIA agent Bryan Mills and his ex-wife Lenore are taken hostage in Istanbul by an Albanian gangster, whose son Bryan killed while bringing down a kidnapping ring. He is able to escape with the aid of his daughter, and together they fight to rescue Lenore and wipe out the criminal family. Action thriller sequel, starring Liam Neeson, Maggie Grace and Famke Janssen(n)

programme.title: Daphne (2017)
programme.desc: A hedonistic London thirtysomething chef has her life thrown off balance when she witnesses a violent crime. British drama, starring Emily Beecham, Geraldine James and Tom Vaughan-Lawlor(n)

programme.title: Solaris (1972)
programme.desc: Psychologist Kris Kelvin is sent to investigate the mysterious events that have taken place on a space station that is orbiting a remote planet and discovers that all but three of the 85 crew are dead and the survivors cannot - or will not - explain what has happened. Andrei Tarkovsky's sci-fi mystery, starring Donatas Banionis, Natalya Bondarchuk and Yuri Yarvet. In Russian(n)

programme.title: The Turning Point (1952)
programme.desc: A journalist helps a crusading district attorney who has been given the task of cracking down on a crime syndicate, but the pair's efforts are hampered by political corruption. Film noir, starring William Holden, Edmond O'Brien and Alexis Smith(n)

programme.title: Esther Waters (1948)
programme.desc: A kitchen maid succumbs to the advances of a caddish stable lad and falls pregnant - but is abandoned for another woman. Period drama, starring Dirk Bogarde and Kathleen Ryan(n)

programme.title: 7th Cavalry (1956)
programme.desc: An army officer is branded a coward for failing to fight in the Battle of the Little Bighorn, and is unable to convince anyone that official business had detained him. In a bid to repair his damaged reputation, he leads a cavalry detail to retrieve the dead from the battlefield. Western, starring Randolph Scott, Jay C Flippen and Jeanette Nolan(n)

programme.title: D-Day the Sixth of June (1956)
programme.desc: A British colonel and an American captain board a ship heading out on a dangerous mission as part of the Normandy Landings. As the two men prepare for the battle to come, they both reminisce about their love for the same woman. Second World War drama, starring Robert Taylor, Richard Todd, Dana Wynter and Edmond O'Brien(n)

programme.title: Everest (2015)
programme.desc: Two expeditions set out to climb to the summit of the world's highest mountain. Both parties of mountaineers get caught in one of the fiercest snowstorms ever recorded, and face a desperate struggle to survive against the elements. Fact-based adventure, starring Jason Clarke, Josh Brolin, Jake Gyllenhaal and Keira Knightley(n)

programme.title: Snowpiercer (2013)
programme.desc: In a snow-ravaged world, a group of travellers fight for survival on a train that perpetually circumnavigates the globe. The vehicle's occupants are divided into a military ruling elite and their dishevelled captives. But now, 17 years after the train set out on its seemingly never-ending journey, the downtrodden, abused captives have had enough. Revolution is afoot. Bong Joon Ho's fantasy thriller, starring Chris Evans, Song Kang-ho, Tilda Swinton, Jamie Bell and Octavia Spencer(n)

programme.title: Rings (2017)
programme.desc: A young woman finds herself on the receiving end of a terrifying curse that threatens to take her life in seven days. Horror, starring Matilda Anna Ingrid Lutz, Alex Roe and Johnny Galecki(n)

programme.title: Submarine (2010)
programme.desc: A 15-year-old boy determined to lose his virginity before he turns 16 becomes infatuated with a feisty classmate. He also has a more pressing mission to undertake as he tries to save his parents' failing marriage, despite his father's apathy and the return of his mother's smooth-talking ex. Comedy drama, with Craig Roberts, Sally Hawkins, Paddy Considine and Noah Taylor(n)

programme.title: Waterloo Road (1944)
programme.desc: A soldier serving in the Second World War discovers his wife has betrayed him by having an affair with a petty crook, so he resolves to settle matters and deserts from the army. He returns to a Blitz-ravaged London to confront his rival, but his quest for retribution is interrupted by an air raid. Drama, starring John Mills, Joy Shelton, Alastair Sim and Stewart Granger(n)

programme.title: The Three Musketeers (1973)
programme.desc: A naive country boy arrives in Paris, keen to seek adventure and prove himself a swordsman. He falls in with a trio of roguish soldiers, and together they set out to save the queen from the schemes of a treacherous cardinal. Swashbuckling adventure, starring Oliver Reed, Michael York, Richard Chamberlain, Frank Finlay, Charlton Heston and Faye Dunaway(n)

programme.title: The Four Musketeers (1974)

Adventure sequel, starring Oliver Reed, Michael York, Charlton Heston, Faye Dunaway, Christopher Lee, Richard Chamberlain and Frank Finlay(n)

Western, starring Edward G Robinson, Barbara Stanwyck and Glenn Ford(n)

Comedy, starring Michael Douglas, Robert De Niro, Morgan Freeman and Kevin Kline(n)

Fantasy adventure, starring Vin Diesel, Elijah Wood, Rose Leslie and Michael Caine(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

Peter and Sharron Sorsky are still reeling from their feedback forms and immediately find fault with the accommodation, and the group is taken to wash bedding the old-fashioned way at the local slate museum(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

They make plans to host weddings on the grounds, while looking ahead to keeping livestock and growing their own crops. Dick takes steps to generate solar power during a hot summer, and turns an old oil tank into a pig roaster(n)

This edition revisits the changing social attitudes reflected on the small screen in the 1960s, including humorous shows which took liberties in the name of satire, disability being mocked and bulletproof vests being put to the test on live TV. With contributions by Joan Bakewell, Peter Purves, Valerie Singleton and Melvyn Bragg(n)

programme.desc: Dave Botha hosts at his Butterfly Paragliding B&B on the Isle of Wight. Deborah Morrison and Wayne Bingham are unhappy they must sleep in a tepee in the garden and use an outdoor toilet, and claim they were served watery eggs and rubbery sausages for breakfast(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Deborah Morrison and Wayne Bingham host the final night at Braddon Hall in Torquay, Devon, where Melvyn and Sindy take issue with their squeaky four-poster bed. The hoteliers try to entertain their guests by inviting them to take part in an archery contest - which ends in accusations of unfair play(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. One of the hosts gets upset about large underpayments and a couple interrogate their guests about the criticism in their feedback, before the winner is announced(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Mr Parsons the chameleon meets a new partner, Daphne from Paris. Jaguars Napo and Goshi are being encouraged to mate, but Nap doesn't seem to be romantically inclined. Young giraffe Murchison faces the great outdoors for the first time, and another baby elephant is born, less than a month after the last new arrival(n)

programme.title: Love It or List It Australia
programme.desc: Andrew Winter and Neale Whitaker meet Kylie and Josh, whose Melbourne home does not meet their current requirements. But while she wants to move, he is keen to renovate(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Presenter Tim Shaw and master mechanic Fuzz Townshend travel to Cornwall in a bid to restore a run-down Triumph Herald that has been housed in a garage for five years(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Fresh from the success of hosting a three-day wedding at the chateau, Dick and Angel make business plans for the grounds, intending to charge people to fish in the moat and forage in the woods. Angel starts work on a second guest suite, but also wants to install an elevator, which pushes Dick's engineering skills to the limit(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of engineer Richard Bennett and his partner Felicia Böhm as they transform an underground water reservoir near the Humber Estuary into a family home. In 2013, the pair decided to embark on the ambitious build with a budget of only £150,000, but it proved a monumental challenge, and design and layout decisions stretched their abilities and the schedule to the max(n)

programme.title: New Amsterdam
programme.desc: Reynolds struggles to help a young cystic fibrosis patient. Sharpe takes a major step in her personal life, and Kapoor runs into Ella again after awkward encounter(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: This edition focuses on the rise in violent crime across the UK and the increase in attacks on police officers. PCs Matt Ambrose and Mike Lowe are dispatched to a house in Crewe following reports of a man threatening his ex-partner with a gun, and later are called to a local sports club where a brawl is underway and they find themselves dangerously outnumbered. PC Claire Heatley arrests a male motorcyclist suspected of drink-driving, but becomes the victim of a vicious assault when the suspect headbutts her(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, with Jon Richardson and Phil Wang taking on guest captain Russell Howard and Roisin Conaty. Nick Helm joins Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner and Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In the concluding part of his trip to the Mill Street Bistro in Norwalk, Ohio, Gordon's disagreement with proprietor Joe Nagy erupts into one of his most intense confrontations yet. Just when it looks like he has turned the corner and is ready to accept help, Joe slips back into his old ways, denying his own failings and picking petty fights. Can Gordon get this stubborn owner to finally accept his advice and secure the future of his restaurant?(n)

programme.title: New Amsterdam
programme.desc: Reynolds struggles to help a young cystic fibrosis patient. Sharpe takes a major step in her personal life, and Kapoor runs into Ella again after awkward encounter(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton visits Vietnam, where a global surge in demand for pepper has transformed the lives of farmers across the country. She learns the difference between black and white varieties and the secrets of peppercorn quality control, and discovers the remarkable lengths to which producers go to guard their valuable crop from thieves. Jimmy Doherty meets a man whose job entails harvesting different strains of yeast from locations around the world and Matt Tebbutt looks into how manufacturers flavour crisps(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Newly-weds Karen and John Leonard from Essex are about to retire. With the last of the six boys they have between them about to leave home, they're looking for a new adventure and a complete change of lifestyle. Scarlette Douglas shows them five properties in the sunny south of Gran Canaria for their budget of £175,000(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Michelle and Gary Cole from Birmingham have both suffered from ill health over the past few years and have decided that the rejuvenating effects of the Spanish sun are just what the doctor ordered. They're looking for a holiday home where they and their two kids can spend quality time together. Laura Hamilton shows them five properties on the Costa Blanca for their £65,000 budget(n)

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig present the 2018 grand final, as the three remaining bakers compete to impress judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith and take home the trophy. For the final three challenges, they must prepare and decorate doughnuts, followed by a deceptively simple technical, and climaxing with an intricate, multi-layered, edible landscape showstopper(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo travel the length of Britain seeking items to fix up, flog on and sell for profit. Max upcycles a brass propeller into a stylish table lamp, while Henry goes all Victorian with an old pond yacht(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel Britain looking for items to fix up and flog on behalf of their owners. In this episode they're close to the Malvern Hills. Henry picks two saddles from horse owner Sabrina and converts them into bar stools, while Simon makes a handsome profit on a 1960s kitchen cupboard(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Simon White and Shirley Boyle, owners of the Old Hall Country House in Madeley, Cheshire, host the week's first round of the B&B competition. The guests are impressed by the 16th-century building, and are taken tap-dancing and to a private fine-dining restaurant, but one of them is dissatisfied with her bedroom's layout and the garnish on her eggs at breakfast(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Eileen Finn invites her fellow contestants to the four-star Thornley House in Allendale, Northumberland, for the second round of the contest, but struggles to follow the impressive first event. The hostess's pet cats are not to all of her guests' liking, and with activities including a tour of a feline cemetery, the theme for the stay is made abundantly clear(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jessica and Adrian Rashleigh are the third hosts, at their three-bedroom establishment Chy Conyn in St Ives, Cornwall. For the activity they arrange a magical mystery camper van tour of the local sights. Some of the guests struggle with the small breakfast room but the food is a success, and Eileen Finn demonstrates her karate skills on the men(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at the Cavendish in Bournemouth, run by Tim and Karen Sturgess. On arrival, the competitors are impressed by the 'pink palace', but things go downhill when a bed is broken and one couple are allocated a room next to the front door. However, Eileen is delighted by the feline memorabilia in her room and the group enjoys a spot of indoor paint-balling and an open-top bus tour of the resort(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. Simon White of Old Hall Country House is surprised by an overpayment and Eileen Finn from the four-star Thornley receives tough criticism, before the winner is announced(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Black rhino Kitani is due to give birth at Chester Zoo and help swell the ranks of this endangered species, but, having lost three previous calves, it is a worrying time and the keepers are concerned that things aren't going to plan. The zoo also has four rare new arrivals called tenrecs, mysterious mammals from Madagascar and, with so little known about them, the keepers try everything they can to help them breed, while red river hog Mali has given birth to twins, but tearaway dad Confetti is a threat to his own babies(n)

programme.title: Love It or List It Australia
programme.desc: Lyndi and Les love where they live and the life they have with their three children in the Adelaide suburb of Frewville. But Les has fallen out of love with their house and feels they have outgrown it. Andrew Winter wants to introduce them to the idea of a move, but Neale Whitaker has some great design ideas to reignite their passion for their current home(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Presenter Tim Shaw and master mechanic Fuzz Townshend head to South Yorkshire to plan to surprise the daughter of an amateur off-road champion by restoring her late mother's Series 1 Land Rover(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree await the arrival of their guests, who are expecting a luxurious Christmas at the chateau. Dick continues work on installing the lift, but the project tests his ingenuity to the limit, while Angel finds more valuables in the attic, including a collection of vinyl records(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A woman is transferred to St George's after crashing her car into a tree, and has to have her broken ankle very quickly realigned if she is to have any hope of walking again. A man who collapsed suddenly is suspected of having a life-threatening head injury, while a four-year-old girl needs treatment after falling on an escalator(n)

programme.title: The Passenger
programme.desc: In Marseille, Mathias is looking for his true identity among the homeless people who know him as Victor Janusz. While there, he discovers a previous murder related to the Greek myth of Icarus. Anais follows Mathias to Marseille and is hot on his heels with the help of Captain Crosnier. Meanwhile, a clandestine meeting regarding a major manipulation of stock prices puts pressure on Anais's father, who has a dark past of his own. Is there a link between the murders and the stock market negotiation? In French. Jean-Hugues Anglade stars. In French(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 92-year-old man may have a bleed on the brain after falling and hitting his head, so is sent for a CT scan. Two patients who came to the UK from Iran are brought in - one suffering from chest pains, the other having difficulty speaking. They and their friends and family share their memories of the revolution and how it affected their lives(n)
